summary: Fix TCP_DELAY
mongo_connect and Mongo should fail if can't connect
description:
connect should make sure the connection can be open and return an error

new Mongo() should fail in the php appropriate way.

summary: memory leak with mongo_insert
description:
behaviour:

if you do several inserts in a row the php process consumes more and more memory,
this happens step wise, however the steps are in no regular interval,
meaning every x inserts the process needs more memory, 
where x in our tests was between 400 and 900 

Script for reproduction:
<?php
$con = mongo_connect("localhost","","",false,false,false); // get connection
$arr = array("test" => "1, 2, 3"); // simple array with data

for($i = 1; $i < 2000000; $i++) // lets do it for 2 million times 
{
  mongo_insert($con, "foo.test", $arr);
  echo "memory usage: " . memory_get_usage(true) ."\n"; // print out current memory usage
}
?>


the resulting output of a run is attached as a file.

summary: Paired Connections
description:
Hello!

I have set up 2 different computers with mongodb installed and running
with no problems - the replica pair way. They both distinguish between
who is master and who is slave and writing to master works perfect.
(http://www.mongodb.org/display/DOCS/Replica+Pairs)

The problem arises with the PHP driver which I have to use in order to
access the database. While the database master IP can change I'm
wondering how to use PHP driver in order to connect to the right
master at all times.

I thought this would work, but it doesn't output any errors of any
kind and it doesn't do anything. I can't see any output or errors in
console (or PHP error log) where mongodb is running.

Everything is set up on OS X 10.5 Leopard Servers.

The code that I use for connecting:
       $m = new Mongo( "IP1:27017,IP2:27017");
       $m->pairConnect();

When I try to connect directly to master mongodb server driver works
OK.

Thank you for helping.

summary: PHP -> JSON can't tell if array() is an empty {} or empty []
$in with empty array throws an error, should it?
description:
As I've understood it using an empty array shouldn't throw an error. Using the below it does.

INPUT
Array pushed to MongoCollection::findOne:

array (
  'oldId' => 
  array (
    '$in' => 
    array (
    ),
  ),
)


OUTPUT
array (
  '$err' => '$in requires array',
)

PHP: MongoCursor does not return MongoID objects but Strings
MongoGridFS::storeFile and MongoGridFS::storeBytes return different things.
description:
These two functions return different things ("Returns if the bytes were saved." vs. "Returns the database id for the file.").
Would be more consistent if they both returned the same thing, since they essentially do the same thing.

http://us2.php.net/manual/en/mongogridfs.storebytes.php
http://us2.php.net/manual/en/mongogridfs.storefile.php

querying specific fields using findOne(array("_id"=>$idUser),array("myfield"=>1)) gives a warning:

Warning: MongoCollection::findOne() expects at most 1 parameter, 2 given

summary: Error in documentation
description:
http://us2.php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.count.php

This page lists that an integer is returned. Then, in the example further down a flot is mentioned as output.

summary: Error checking always increases memory usage
description: On every Mongo::lastError execution the memory footprint increases. I tested to reduce memory with Mongo::resetError. But this needs even more memory. I have a complex PHP script which needs without error checking about 2MB and with error checking on every insert, update and save (it's about 500000 operations) it needs about 105MB. Nothing else changed. It seems (after some tests) that every error checking operation (also indirectly with MongoDB::execute) increases memory usage of the PHP process (memory_get_usage reports increased memory usage after every operation). Is there any workaround i've overseen.

summary: PHP driver produces "getMore: cursorid not found" in mongod output
description:
The following test script produces the error "Sat Aug 29 20:36:34 getMore: cursorid not found test.testcollection 0."  The equivalent JS script does not.

PHP:

$mongo = new Mongo();                                                                               

$collection = $mongo->selectDB("test")->selectCollection("testcollection");                         

$collection->drop();                                                                                
                                                                                                    
$collection->save(array("name" => "Foo"));                                                          

$cursor = $collection->find(array("name" => "Foo"));                                                

$cursor->hasNext();                                                                                 
$cursor->getNext();                                                                                 
$cursor->hasNext();                                                                                 

JS:

db.testcollection.drop();                                                                           

db.testcollection.save({ name: "Foo" });                                                            

var c = db.testcollection.find({ name: "Foo" });                                                    

c.hasNext();                                                                                        
c.next();                                                                                           
c.hasNext();

summary: $ conflicts with php's $, a pretty significat distraction for php developers
description:
Hi

I'm sure this has been raised several times but I figured what the heck.

The $, can we have that renamed to different symbol like @, that $ dollar sign is php's main symbol for variables.
It really bothers me because I'd like to have mongo just have a beautiful language that works with php. I like everything about mongo just except that $ which messes it all up in the code-uniformity department with php.

Any thoughts?

Connection timeout should be configurable
description:
Currently, the php driver tries to connect to the specified host and timeouts in 20 seconds, which is hardcoded.

It would be good if this limit was configurable by passing it as an option to the constructor (as milliseconds or something, in case someone wants it to timeout in less than a second).
If nothing is passed, it could use the `default_socket_timeout` configuration option.

Using pairs/sets of servers and one of them failing causes a huge slowdown now, because of that 20 second timeout.

Extraneous output
The MongoCollection docs need to mention $
description:
Collection names can use any character in the ASCII set per the PHP manual that's not so, it's the ASCII set minus $. I tested this with the shell but also the PECL mongodb source code for listCollections reflects this as it checks for the character in a system.namespaces entry to see that it's not an index: // check that the ns is valid and not an index (contains $).

How can the ns be invalid, I am curious? Someone manually writes into system.namespaces?

summary: Double free in php_mongo_write_update, php_mongo_write_delete etc
Using $set with nested array does not work
description:
<?php
$m = new Mongo();
$db = $m->selectDB('checkout');
$c = $db->selectCollection('merchant');

$c->insert(array('_id'=>'testid', 'config'=>array()));
print_r($c->findOne(array('_id'=>'testid')));

$c->update(array('_id'=>'testid'), array('$set'=>array('config'=>array('test_test'=>array('array_test'=>null))), array('upsert'=>true)));

print_r($c->findOne(array('_id'=>'testid')));
?>

This should update the config array but it does not.  I think the code should be self-explanatory.

Here is the output on my machine

Array
(
    [_id] => testid
    [config] => Array
        (
        )

)

Array
(
    [_id] => testid
    [config] => Array
        (
        )

)

summary: Call to undefined method MongoId::__set_state()
description:
$_REQUEST=iterator_to_array(<MongoCursor>,FALSE); // <<-- works fine

But the PHP global receiving the array is not always $_REQUEST. PHP doesn't allow variable variables involving globals either, so eval is the only option here.

eval('$_'.$phpglobal.'=iterator_to_array(<MongoCursor>,FALSE);'); <<-- generates the MongoId::__set_state() error

summary: utf8 detection doesn't work properly
MongoGridFS::remove just_one option removes more than one file's chunks
Segfault crashes webserver on Mongo constructor invocation.
description:
Doing a very simple connection in index page on a new install, mongo shell works normally.

   $mongo = new Mongo();     // works fine
   $mongo = new Mongo("mongodb://name:password@localhost/") crashes the server thread.

Valgrind gives the following stack trace:

==25033== Invalid read of size 4
==25033==    at 0x6201FEC: zim_MongoDB_authenticate (db.c:502)
==25033==    by 0x61EF2C0: php_mongo_do_socket_connect (mongo.c:2136)

The line there in question is:

  if (zend_hash_find(HASH_P(result), "nonce", strlen("nonce")+1, (void**)&nonce) == SUCCESS) {


I don't know Zend well enough to even understand what's going on there, but I will try to mess with it and suggest a patch.

This is blocking all work, obviously.  Strange that I'm getting this and others aren't - seems like a simple fencepost!

summary: MongoCursor::count argument name is superb confusing
description: Calling this $all is very confusing given that "On a query using skip() and limit(), count ignores these parameters by default.  Use count(true) to have it consider the skip and limit values in the calculation" -- isnt this the exact opposite of being "all"? Ie if the argument is TRUE then it counts limited and if it's FALSE then it counts all found.

summary: Constants for INC/DESC ordering of indexes
description: I think it would make sense for code readability to have Mongo::INC and Mongo::DESC constants that represent 1/-1, for index definitions.

summary: Don't have GridFS ensureIndex when reading from a slave
zero-length key should throw exception
description: Protects against "$foo" mistakes

summary: Ability to connect using a socket file
description: It would help to be able to connect to mongo using a socket file.

summary: Driver should not throw transport endpoint exception when at least some rs servers are reachable
description:
When connecting to mongodb://a,b with replicaSet => yes, where 'a' is down, we get the following exception

(10297)exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'mongodb://a:27018: Transport endpoint is not connected'

This seems like less than ideal behavior, since b has been voted primary and all writes/reads can still succeed. It's possible to catch this, parse out the dead server and remove it from the pool, but that seems like a less than ideal situation, especially for cases where servers may come up or down intermittently. In particular, removing nodes in this way may result in a configuration where no seeds are reachable. Seems like the driver should handle these cases.

summary: Make properties of exceptions accessible
description:
This is really two issues, but they are related and can probably be fixed at the same time. Please let me know if you want me to break this up into multiple bugs.

When inserting into a collection, if the record already exists (say due to a race condition leading to it being inserted elsewhere), I would like to be able to detect that the record was already inserted elsewhere and handle it (say by reading the existing data).

Mongo throws a MongoCursorException when you try to insert a record with an existing _id. There are other issues that can trigger exceptions that will need to be handled in some way other than reading, so I need to differentiate between the exceptions. Unfortunately, right now all properties including code are either protected or private, so it's impossible to determine the cause of the exception. 

The other problem is that there doesn't seem to be a code that means "tried to insert duplicate _id".  In my testing the code in MongoCursorException (according to var_dump, only way I could find to get the information) is 0, which according to the docs means " You are calling a method that sets up the query after executing the query. Reset the cursor and try again."

So in summary:
1. Need access to code in MongoCursorException

2. Need a code in MongoCursorException that means "tried to insert duplicate _id"

Let me know if I should split this up.

Thanks!

summary: GridFS segmentation fault error if a NULL property is passed
Unable to getCode() on MongoCursorException
description:
$e->getCode() on a MongoCursorException outputs 0 which makes the codes impossible to check.

A comment on the docs suggests this is because all the methods are private - http://uk3.php.net/manual/en/class.mongocursorexception.php#99508

summary: 64-bit int gets truncated by PHP-MongoDB driver
Bad encoding of integers in
description:
Running "print_r(bin2hex(bson_encode(1)));" in PHP outputs 00000001, instead of 01000000.
We can conclude that when the host architecture is big-endian, encoding is not converted to little-endian, as it should.

summary: The Mongo::connect method returns null
Lighttpd/FastCGI: persistent connection creates one connection per worker process (replicaSet enabled)
description:
When multiple worker processes are used with Lighttpd/FastCGI, a persistent connection creates one connection per process.  

The replicaSet option is enabled for the connection, not sure if that contributes to the problem.

Setup:

1) Lighttpd/FastCGI configured to use one controller and 10 worker processes.  /etc/lighttpd/conf-available/10-fastcgi.conf contains:

server.modules   += ( "mod_fastcgi" )

fastcgi.debug = 1  

fastcgi.server    = (
        ".php" =>
        ((
                "bin-path" => "/usr/bin/php-cgi",
                "socket" => "/tmp/php.socket",
                "min-procs" => 1,
                "max-procs" => 1,  
                "idle-timeout" => 20,
                "bin-environment" => (
                        "PHP_FCGI_CHILDREN" => "10",
                        "PHP_FCGI_MAX_REQUESTS" => "10000"
                ),
                "bin-copy-environment" => (
                        "PATH", "SHELL", "USER"
                ),
                "broken-scriptfilename" => "enable",
                "check-local" => "disable"
        ))
)


2) php script uses a hard-coded id string for the persistent connection:

  $instance_name = "SOMESTRING";
  $mongo_servers = '172.16.0.1,172.16.0.2,172.16.0.3';
  $connection = new Mongo("mongodb://$mongo_servers", array("replicaSet" => true, "persist" => (string)$instance_name));


Expectation:

Mongo should show one open connection for the web server, as the same server and id string combination is used for each persistent connection.

See:
http://php.net/manual/en/mongo.connecting.php
http://php.net/manual/en/mongo.construct.php


Results:

After running the php script several thousand times using a stress test tool, the REST administrator interface on the Mongo server shows 20 connections related to the web server.  Two connections per process ( I assume that the second is for replication ), so I expect to see 2 connections for the web server instead of 20.

The connections appear in pairs:

Client	OpId		Op		Namespace		Query			Client
conn	8357401	2004	?documents		{ getlasterror: 1 }	172.16.0.143:52766		
conn	0			0										(NONE)

summary: Count doesn't use slave in replicaset configuration
Large numbers / integers are silently mangled
Strict comparison of two MongoIDs fails
description:
The problem arises with strict comparison of two separate MongoIds, though it could be me being a donut.

I'm grabbing a collection from the database and storing it in $this->_user (so the user's id is in $this->_user->_id). Now I'm grabbing some work that the user has made, where the MongoID of the user is stored in ($work->user). If I strictly compare $this->_user->_id to $work->user it throws FALSE, despite it being exactly the same MongoID. Echoing them out shows exactly the same HEX string and querying the two documents in the Mongo shell shows them stored as ObjectID's with the same hash.

Anything I'm doing wrong?

summary: ensureIndex shouldn't allow name > 128 chars
MongoException - zero-length keys are not allowed, did you use $ with double quotes?
description:
We recently updated the php mongo driver from 1.0.6 to 1.0.10 and experienced these "zero-length keys" MongoExceptions while saving documents like

array('data' => array ('' => 'all', 'k' => 'v'))

We wonder why the driver complains about the empty key since mongo and other drivers (e.g. pymongo 1.9) allow the storage of documents containing empty keys

> db.coll.insert({data: {"": "all", k: "v"}})
> db.coll.find()
{ "_id" : ObjectId("4cc1bfa097e7872d250248dc"), "data" : { "" : "all", "k" : "v" } } 

Is this an issue or something we have to live with?

Markus

summary: Out of memory when coonectintg to non existing host in replica set
type -92 not supported on MongoCursorTimeoutException
description:
We have error_reporting(0) and display_error to 0 but when mongo throw MongoCursorTimeoutException exception somtimes error messages is printed to output like this:

type -92 not supported
56 103 126 -75 -92 -76 109 8 25 0
type -92 not supported
32 54 124 -75 -92 -76 109 8 17 0

summary: Update documentation to reflect issues with iterator_to_array and collections without _id fields
Call to undefined method MongoCursor::batch()
description:
Mongo produces the following fatal error when trying to use the batch() function.

Fatal error:  Call to undefined method MongoCursor::batch() in /var/www/system/application/models/driver/mongo_m.php on line 651

The code:

$tmp 	= $collection->find($filter)->sort(array($sort['by'] => $sort['order']))->limit($sort['limit'])->batch($sort['batch']);

We tried various ways and syntaxes to run this code and all resulted in the error. We concluded this is a bug because  replacing the batch() function with batchSize()  executes without fatal exceptions.

summary: Deadlock when PHP is forked
description:
This is a rather small corner case. I haven't been able to isolate it 100% because it is not reliable, but it seems that when forking processes using pcntl_fork() and using persistent Mongo connections, eventually one or more of the processes will get blocked waiting on recv forever and ever, even if a new connection is created in the child process. There are no notices or assertions in the Mongo daemon when this occurs.

summary: Document fields are "unset" if no $set modifier is passed when updating.
description:
When I do an update with no data, all fields are unset on the specified document. Additionally, when I do an update with any modifier besides $set, all fields that were unaffected by the update are unset from the document. If I use $set, even on a single field, then all fields are left alone and only that field is modified. So basically, if $set is not passed, then all unaffected fields are unset.

Since there is an $unset modifier, I would assume that even if I passed no data to the update that nothing should happen. Same as if I only atomically update certain fields - say to increment, or add-to-set - then all other fields should be left alone unless explicitly using the $unset modifier.

This is an issue with the PHP driver and not the MongoDB shell. The shell behaves as expected.

Failover impossible with 2 nodes
description:
Hi

I can't make the failover work with my replica set of 2 local nodes.
I get this error  :

from the PHP code : 
$connection = new Mongo("mongodb://localhost:27018,mongodb://localhost:27017", array("replicaSet" => true)); 

My test case : 
I run 2 nodes + an arbiter.
I shutdown the secondary : connection is still OK.
I restart the secondary.
I shutdown the primary.

My config :

           }


What I am missing ? I tried a few config options (priority, no votes, etc) but nothing helped :(

Thanks !

summary: MongoTimestamp issues.
description:
2 Primary issues with MongoTimestamp in PHP driver.

1st) Other drivers can use the MongoTimestamp to create a server side timestamp as described in this post:

http://groups.google.com/group/mongodb-user/browse_thread/thread/30ebf2bd8d20494d/f412dcc097291f19

I am trying to do this with PHP driver and get odd results:

with:
'ts'=>new MongoTimestamp() 

I get something weird like this: 
{ "_id" : ObjectId("4d28ad978ead0e6f36000008"), "ts" : { "t" : 3000, "i" : 1294511511 } } 

NOTE: t and i seem to be backwards, and also this is not the server side generated stamp.

'ts'=>new MongoTimestamp(0) 

produces:
{ "_id" : ObjectId("4d28ae4a8ead0e6c3600000a"), "ts" : { "t" : 3000, "i" : 0 } } 

But I found that I can do this: 
'ts'=>new MongoTimestamp(array()) 
..and it works. 
{ "_id" : ObjectId("4d28ae738ead0e6e3600000b"), "ts" : { "t" : 1294511731000, "i" : 1 } } 

BUT, it throws a php warning of: 

Warning: MongoTimestamp::__construct() expects parameter 1 to be long, 
array given in... 

when creating the MongoTimestamp.  This can be supressed like this:

@$row=array('ts'=>new MongoTimestamp(array())); 

..but thats not an ideal solution.


2nd)  Retrieving the timestamp object yields reversed results.


{ "_id" : "stime", "ts" : { "t" : 1294512437000, "i" : 1 } } 

$val=$tcoll->findOne(array('_id'=>'stime')); 
print_r($val); 

Array ( [_id] => stime [ts] => MongoTimestamp Object ( [sec] => 1 [inc] => 1294512437 ) ) 

...and so the __toString provides the increment rather than the sec since it is mapped to the wrong field.

- Thanks!

summary: heap overflow detected warning on 1.1.2 Driver with Suhosin Patch
recv calls in mongo.c are not checking for return value of 0
description:
I experienced an error in the v1.0.8 PHP driver where the client's 
connection to mongos has been closed by the peer (due to a crash of 
some sort on the mongodb server), but the driver is infinitely looping 
in the php_mongo_get_reply() function.  It is making constant 
recvfrom() calls with varying sizes (4, 20, 66) and gets a return 
value of 0 from each call but continues to make these calls anyways. 
(Determined using strace, gdb, and lsof.)

I looked at the v1.0.8 mongo.c code in the PHP driver tarball; available here: 
https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/tarball/1.0.8

The bug was pretty obvious: the calls to recv were being checked for
a return of FAILURE (defined as -1), but not for a return value of 0.
Even though the connection code was rewritten between 1.0.8 and 
1.1.3, the calls to recv are still not having their results checked for a 
return value of 0.

So I propose that the calls to recv in mongo.c be changed to check 0 
as well as FAILURE and treat them both the same.


FYI, here is some more information on the behavior I encountered 
in the v1.0.8 driver:

...
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 20, 0, NULL, NULL)   = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 66, 0, NULL, NULL)   = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 20, 0, NULL, NULL)   = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 66, 0, NULL, NULL)   = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 4, 0, NULL, NULL)    = 0
recvfrom(4, ""..., 20, 0, NULL, NULL)   = 0
...

These are the only system calls being executed by this process.

The return value of 0 indicates that "the peer has performed an orderly shutdown." (According to recvfrom manpage).
So the process is just constantly polling a socket whose peer no longer exists.

When I look at the OS's view of what socket fd 4 of this process is connected to, it shows that the OS doesn't know about this socket anymore:

% sudo lsof -p 9166 | grep 4u
php     9166 foo_username    4u  sock    0,4          268571076 can't identify protocol


This happened at the same time that the mongod process crashed.

summary: M/R raises a DB assert if output parameter is not specified.
description:
According to http://www.mongodb.org/display/DOCS/MapReduce, 
"For versions of MongoDB greater than 1.7.4, the output options have changed. Map-reduce will no longer generate temporary collections (so the keepTemp has been removed). Now, you must always supply a value for out"
Therefore MongoDB 1.8.1 is not really backward compatible with 1.6.x ... and the example at http://www.php.net/manual/en/mongodb.command.php should be updated or annotated !

summary: Querying from MapReduce generated collection with sub-object in _id field
description:
I ran map reduce and used sub-objects in my key field -  e.g. {_id:{keyOne:1, keyTwo:2}} 
Now when I attempt to query on a sub-object in the _id field - e.g. $collection->find(array('_id.keyOne'=>1))   it returns nothing. If I run the exact same query from the terminal, it works great.
It also works when I run the query with no criteria and correctly gives me the _id field with an array of values in PHP.
It also works to use the dot notation for other values in the reduced table, just not any sub-objects in the _id field of the document.

summary: $eq Operator throws Exception
description:
The following command works in MongoDB itself: { 'categories.id': { $eq: 247 } }

In PHP the query throws the following exception:

 PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'invalid operator: $eq' 

Mongo says: assertion 10068 invalid operator: $eq ns:db.collection query:{ $query: { categories.id: { $eq: 247 } }


I don't get that there's a problem in the code. The request array in PHP is

Array ( [categories.id] => Array ( [$eq] => 247 ) )


Seems to be a bug, doesn't it?

summary: Mongo date query times out
slaveOk queries being routed to RECOVERING nodes in a replicaset
description: I'm unsure if this is a server issue or driver issue, but we noticed that slaveOk queries are being routed to recovering nodes in a replicaset. These queries fail of course with 'not master or secondary, can't read' exception. Queries probably shouldn't be routed to nodes whose states are not appropriate for answering queries.

summary: rs connection should use ismaster, not replsetgetstatus
connecting and reading from a degraded replicationSet should be possible when slaveOkay
description:
We could not read from replicaSet when rebooting the replicationMaster (even with slaveOkay=true).
Fixing this is the main motiviation behind this report, however the problem only occurs for a few seconds during restarts.

So I came up with a similar but static scenario:
I'm trying to connect against a replicaSet wich has no master (only 1 of 3 nodes up)

Using the mongo shell it is possible to query the remaining server for data (db.getMongo().setSlaveOkay())
Doing the same with the php client fails with MongoConnectionException 'Couldn't determine master'

Supporting this might improve the availability of websites during server failure as a similar thing happens when stopping the current master and the remaining servers have not yet held an election.

summary: setting replicaSet option (even to false) creates rs connection
Connection pools are not cleaned out properly
description: On failover and restart w/ multiple connections

summary: pecl extension doesn't compile on OSX 10.7 Lion (GM)
description:
On 10.7, PPC support was completely dropped.
"mongo" pecl extension, thus, fails to compile because it's trying to find PPC compilers.

I managed to workaround the issue by removing:
-arch ppc -arch ppc64
from the configure script, after running "phpize"

summary: PHP 0-based array stored as object in MongoDB
Executing queries containing command modifier using $db->execute() or $db->command(array('$eval' => $code) not working
description: Basic queries/commands work but any time I use modifiers such as $or, $in, etc. the queries seem to do nothing when they execute, give no error and nothing is returned.  Discussion and example can be found at: http://groups.google.com/group/mongodb-user/browse_thread/thread/2cd1381faec91d59

summary: Persistent Connections Inhibit Failover
description:
I've been testing failover and measuring 
data loss when asking a primary replica to step down. I've setup a 
very simple test as follows: 

1) Setup a PHP script that increments a count using findandmodify 
2) Use ab (apache benchmark) to do 10,000 requests with a concurrency of 5 
3) Tell the primary replica to step down during this process 
4) Look at the final value of the counter 

Ideally, the counter final value would be 10,000. I've tested this two 
ways. First with the PHP driver's persistent connections turned on and 
second without persistent connections. Previous discussions on the 
list have indicated that mongos does its own connection pooling, so 
with persistent connections turned on, you're essentially pooling a 
pool. Long story short, with persistent connections turned on, and PHP 
talking to mongos, there's a lot of data loss. 

The final count generally ends up around 2,200 while it should be 
10,000. Turning them off, the final count is generally in the ballpark 
of 9980 or 9990 (very close to where it should be). A bizarre side 
effect of this is that connecting mongo to mongos with the persistent 
connections turned on frequently gives a "not master" message as if 
the stale pooled PHP connections are somehow influencing new 
connections as well. No such behavior is present when turning of PHP's 
persistent connections.

summary: Persistent connection issues with 1.2.1
description:
We've been using non-persistent connections to get around some issues with the driver.  I noticed that lots of persistent connection fixes have gone into the latest release, so I decided to give it a shot.  Here's what I'm seeing.

First, the test script:
# cat test.php 
<?php
$m = new Mongo("mongodb://localhost/");
$db = $m->selectDB("ehow");
$c = $db->selectCollection("en.comment");

$data = $c->findOne(Array("legacy_id" => 32933));
var_dump($data);

?>

summary: Use persistent connections by default
description: Mongo::__construct() does not use persistent connections by default. If using persistent connections is a best practice and there is no problem, the PHP driver should use persistent connections by default.

problem
Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'Interrupted system call' under stress / slow query 

----
It appears caused by unhandled EINTR signal. I made a patch for mongo-php-driver and works fine

// mongo.c
static int get_header(int sock, mongo_cursor *cursor TSRMLS_DC) {
...
+select_retry:
      status = select(sock+1, &readfds, NULL, &exceptfds, &timeout);
#ifdef EINTR
+      if (status == -1 && errno == EINTR) 
+        goto select_retry;   
#endif

summary: Insert throws an exception in php driver 1.2.3 in a sharding config
description:
PHP no longer inserts in a sharding configuration in version 1.2.3. (1.2.2 is fine)

Before insert the driver seems to execute the command _isSelf, a command that mongos doesn't know. I traced the call to the function mongo_util_server__other_le in user/server.c

summary: Gridfs driver doesn't fill in length field on empty files
Change BSON bin type from 2 to 0
description: reads 2 or 0, but write 0.

summary: Add TCP connection information to MongoCursorException messages
description:
We've been having numerous MongoCursorExceptions occurring, but it's been very hard to correlate these client-side errors with the mongos connections that are presumably failing.  It would be immensely helpful to include full TCP connection info in the exception message.  i.e., <local-ip>:<local-port> to <remote-ip>:<remote-port>.
That way I can figure out which mongos connection had the MongoCursorException occur on it.

Examples of mongo exceptions we have seen many times:

PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorTimeoutException' with message 'cursor timed out (timeout: 30000, time left: 0:0, status: 0)' 
PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'Operation now in progress' 
PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'couldn't get response header'

We've also seen many of these exceptions, though they are not MongoCursorExceptions:

PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoException' with message 'type 0x87 not supported: 58 3e e7 85 87 9e 27 06 00'

summary: refs are weird
MongoDriver allows PHP code to create and use stats collection in database
description:
It is possible to create and work with collection called "stats", which results in errors such as weird behavior of map-reduce. 

PHP MongoDriver should throw an exception when collection "stats" is used.

summary: exception: JS_NewObject failed for global
ClusteredCursor::query not throwing an Exception
description:
In an attempt to repro SUPPORT-88, I found the following issue with the PHP Driver.

When the PHP cursor encounters a "ClusteredCursor::query" exception from the server it does not throw an exception. Instead it returns an array containing the [$err] & [code]
Array
(
    [$err] => ns: test.test ClusteredCursor::query ShardConnection had to change
    [code] => 9996
)

This seems unusual. The Python driver *does* throw the exception.

====
Attached files:
 1.  support-88.php -> file used to generate the error
 2. support-88-output_php.txt -> output from this file.
====

====
To repro:
 1. Start a sharded config.
 2. Ensure the 'test.test' is has a shard key of _ID
 3. Update .php file to point to the mongoS
 4. Run the PHP file

This should:
 1. Create 500k entries
 2. Cursor through those entries and modify a 'text' field
 3. This change will cause the cursor to loop through more than 500k items (expected)
 4. At some point you will get a "ClusteredCursor" error
====

If you look at the output, you'll see the following:
Bad Data
Array
(
    [$err] => ns: test.test ClusteredCursor::query ShardConnection had to change
    [code] => 9996
)

Notice how that code is not the "try/catch" it's actually a test for "is this data valid?"

summary: Under multiple threads php mongo driver Segfaults while attempting to free link with php_mongo_free_cursor_le
description:
Under load against the webserver the php-mongo driver cores via a segfault when attempting to free a link. Looks like it may be attempting to free a link that is no longer there resulting in this condition. Final signal and gdb backtrace below. This was happening in 1.2.4 of the driver and we compiled 1.2.6 of the driver with the same result. Single connections seem to work fine, it is when we generate multiple simultaneous connections from multiple web threads that result in the segfault from the driver. summary: Storing files in dotted collections crashes mongodb
description:
The following code makes the PHP driver receive a "PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'couldn't send command'", at which point the database process crashes:

<?php
$m = new Mongo();
$db = $m->selectDB("gridfs_test");
$grid = $db->getGridFS('something.dotted'); //critical part here
$id = $grid->storeBytes('abc', array('my' => 'metadata'));
?>

When a non-dotted collection name is used, everything works fine.

The same code works without crashes on mongodb 2.0 with mongo-php-driver 1.1.0.
Similar code works without crashes on mongodb 2.0 with pymongo 2.0.1.

The database log contains the following:

summary: cursor timeout does not work correctly
description:
While using MongoCollection::drop() and MongoCollection::findOne() methods, MongoCursorTimeoutException is always thrown, even the timeout is not reached yet. For example, with timeout=50000, the exception is still thrown after few milliseconds.

PHP code for testing:
http://mongo.pastebin.com/bHn3u7CN

Script output:
starting test at [2010-08-31 12:58:34]
object(Mongo)#2 (3) {
  ["connected"]=>
  bool(true)
  ["server:protected"]=>
  string(35) "mongodb://10.36.15.153,10.36.15.152"
  ["persistent:protected"]=>
  NULL
}
lets drop the collection now
exception [MongoCursorTimeoutException] occurred after 0.00014710426330566 seconds
cursor timed out (50000 ms)
lets insert test values
lets find the value now
exception [MongoCursorTimeoutException] occurred after 0.00024700164794922 seconds
cursor timed out (50000 ms)



If the timeout is disabled (MongoCursor::$timeout=-1) it works correctly.

Important information: on PHP 5.2.10 server, with PHP driver 1.0.8, it works fine!!! (machine info: CentOS release 4.7 (Final), Linux carloss-bo.devel 2.6.9-78.0.13.ELxenU #1 SMP Wed Jan 14 16:10:05 EST 2009          x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux)

summary: documentation for getIndexInfo
description: The info at http://ca.php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.getindexinfo.php regarding getIndexInfo seems to be outdated. In particular, the function doesn't return " a list of index names", but a list of associative arrays describing the indexes ...

summary: update, insert, remove docs should mention extra fields on return when safe=true
PHP Driver connects to the arbiters?
description:
It seems that the PHP Driver parses and connects to the arbiters as if they were regular hosts/servers. Not sure why this is the case though, is this really needed?

The case we ran into is that we had 2 arbiters running along with the replica set and the arbiter ports were not open via firewall, we observed 80sec+ connect time (which was successful in the end).

I thought arbiters were only for Mongo server voting?

The code in question: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/master/util/rs.c#L331

Please advise.

summary: MongoDate in PHP conversion
Leaking connections to mongod for replicaSet => true
description:
After a while of working, we have tons of connections to mongo, seen by netstat. 

 lsof | grep php5-cgi | grep sock | awk '{ a[$1 $2] ++ }END{for(i in a){ print i , "\t", a[i] }}'

Gets ~20 connections for ~200 requests (we have limited each php process to re-spawn after 200 requests completed).

summary: Segfault in PHP driver on CLI
description:
I've been trying to run a data import script from the PHP CLI and consistently getting segfaults. The script is processing around 500,000 records and creates a segmentation fault about an hour in. I've had this happen on a Debian Squeeze server (running the 1.2.3 driver) and my Linux Mint box (running 1.2.6 driver).

The project is using Shanty Mongo on Zend Framework. The import script is creating around 80,000 entries and then going through around 400,000 key/value pairs from a relational database and appending them to these entries.

gdb backtrace of php (from local machine):

Program received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
0x000000000069a0f0 in ?? ()
#0  0x000000000069a0f0 in ?? ()
#1  0x000000000069b721 in zend_parse_parameters ()
#2  0x00007ffff490378a in zim_MongoCursor___construct (ht=<value optimised out>, return_value=0x7fffffffbdf0, return_value_ptr=<value optimised out>, this_ptr=0x1f0df28,

    return_value_used=<value optimised out>) at /tmp/pear/temp/mongo/collection.c:493
#4  0x00007ffff490b9fe in zim_MongoCollection_findOne (ht=2, return_value=0x1f0f408, return_value_ptr=<value optimised out>, this_ptr=0x1f0fa10, 
    return_value_used=<value optimised out>) at /tmp/pear/temp/mongo/collection.c:509
#5  0x00000000006de59d in ?? ()

#10 0x00007ffff55f5eff in __libc_start_main () from /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6
#11 0x0000000000428859 in _start ()

I'm not 100% sure if there isn't some bad data causing this, (there's around half a million entries that each ) but even bad data shouldn't be causing a segfault. If I find some bad data that's causing it, I'll add it to this issue.

summary: Can't find Javascript value with operator $type
ext/mongo classes should return meaningful results from Reflection API
Replica Set reads don't distribute evenly across nodes
description:
We have 7 app servers connecting to a Replica Set with 3 secondaries.  One would expect that each of the secondaries would get about the same number of queries using slaveOK calls.  However, one of our secondaries (the first in the seed list) gets double the number of queries of the other 2 secondaries combined.

Aside from randomizing the seed list on each server, is there any way we could make the driver be smarter about picking which secondary it sticks to, so that load is better distributed across the servers?

summary: listCollections
getHosts() not working with 1.2.7
description:
MongoConnection::getHosts() is not returning information on the other machines in the cluster, it's only returning info on the local machine.

This script:

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
$m = new Mongo('127.0.0.1', array('replicaSet' => true));
var_dump($m->getHosts());
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

produces this:

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
array(1) {
  ["127.0.0.1:27017"]=>
  array(4) {
    ["health"]=>
    int(0)
    ["state"]=>
    int(0)
    ["ping"]=>
    int(2147483647)
    ["lastPing"]=>
    int(0)
  }
}
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

which looks like bogus data -- the machine is definitely up and running. This script:

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
$m = new Mongo('nz1.wb.gs:27017', array('replicaSet' => true));
var_dump($m->getHosts());
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

produces this:

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
array(1) {
  ["nz1.wb.gs:27017"]=>
  array(4) {
    ["health"]=>
    int(1)
    ["state"]=>
    int(2)
    ["ping"]=>
    int(0)
    ["lastPing"]=>
    int(1326227008)
  }
}
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

which is accurate data (it's a secondary), but it should include information on the other machines in the replica set.

I've run the same script on the primary and the secondary and I get the same result. It also makes no difference using the replica set name (wgnz) instead of true in the options array.

The replica set itself is running fine, I've attached a screenshot of the web status page, which is actually publicly available here:

http://nz1.wb.gs:28017/_replSet
http://nz2.wb.gs:28017/_replSet

Also, rs.status() from the command line returns correct information on all the machines in the cluster.

My PHP check script is available here:

http://admin.nz.wb.gs/mongo.php

and I've made it print the source so you can see what it's doing.

This worked fine until I upgraded from 1.2.6 to 1.2.7.

<?php
$bytes = file_get_contents('some-file.zip');
$m = new Mongo('192.168.0.100'); // i.e. a remote host
$db = $m->test;
$grid = $db->getGridFS();
$saved = $grid->storeBytes(
	$bytes,
	array(
		'filename' => 'test_file-'.rand(0,10000),
		'size' => 'm',
		'otherdata' => 'BIG'
	),
	array('safe' => true)
);

?>

summary: getGridFS followed by storeBytes hangs request indefinitely
description:
Several endpts in our app that previously worked with driver version 1.2.7 now hang indefinitely in 1.2.9.  Rolling back fixed the problem completely.

The request never seems to finish and no errors are output to the apache logs.  Commenting out all GridFS logic fixed the problem.

The endpoints in question just took image data from the $_FILES superglobal using gd, resized them, and saved them down to a collection called 'tmp' in GridFS.  I am uncertain how to get more information about what's going wrong since nothing is output to the logs.  Attached is the relevant code.

summary: 1.2.8/1.2.9 don't compile with PHP < 5.3
description: Right now, the driver doesn't compile for PHP < 5.3 due to undefined symbols Z_DELREF_P.

summary: MongoCursorTimeoutException causes connections not to close
MongoCursorException with Apache while master steps down
description:
Script to reproduce:

<?php

$mongo = new \Mongo(
        'mongodb://localhost:27017', array ('replicaSet' => 'foo')
);

$db = $mongo->testdb;
$coll = $db->selectCollection('testcollection');
$value = rand (0, 1234567);
$coll->insert(array('foo' => $value), array ('safe' => true));

echo 'Inserted ' . $value;
?>

When I open following script via browser, data gets inserted to database as it should be.

Now I do this:

[root@studiodev ~]# mongo --port 27017
MongoDB shell version: 2.0.1
connecting to: 127.0.0.1:27017/test
PRIMARY> rs.stepDown(1);
{
        "closest" : NumberLong(1329909335),
        "difference" : NumberLong(0),
        "ok" : 1
}
SECONDARY>

So my other mongod instance on port 27018 becomes primary:

[root@studiodev ~]# mongo --port 27018
MongoDB shell version: 2.0.1
connecting to: 127.0.0.1:27018/test
PRIMARY>

Now I open this same PHP script via browser first time I get:

Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'couldn't determine master' in /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php on line 10

MongoCursorException: couldn't determine master in /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php on line 10

Call Stack:
    0.0000     626144   1. {main}() /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php:0
    0.0001     631024   2. MongoCollection->insert() /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php:10

Or this:

Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'not master' in /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php on line 10

MongoCursorException: not master in /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php on line 10

Call Stack:
    0.0000     626144   1. {main}() /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php:0
    0.0001     631184   2. MongoCollection->insert() /var/www/secure_html/crasman/mongotest.php:10

Or sometimes something like (about every other page reload):

Inserted 9560

And nothing is written to collection when exception is thrown.

When I restart apache and start to reload my script with browser, no errors occur and everything is written to DB. Same happens if I restart apache after saying rs.stepDown(1) to primary mongo node (=everything is written to DB, no exceptions).

I think the problem is related to PHP driver as I can connect and perform other operations using MongoVUE client.

summary: improve MongoCursor->batchSize documentation
description: The MongoCursor->batchSize documentation doesn't seem very clear to me (http://ca3.php.net/manual/en/mongocursor.batchsize.php). In particular, it doesn't detail de difference between positive and negative values. The documentation coming with the Java driver seems better (http://api.mongodb.org/java/2.6/com/mongodb/DBCursor.html#batchSize(int))

summary: Apache httpd throws error with php_mongo-1.2.10-5.4-vc9.dll
description:
Tried to install MongoDB PHP driver
Copied php_mongo-1.2.10-5.4-vc9.dll to .\xampp\php\ext
Added to php.ini: extension=php_mongo-1.2.10-5.4-vc9.dll
Started Apache on Port 8080
Apache threw windows error dialog: The procedure entry point zend_new_interned_string could not be located in the dynamic link library php5ts.dll

summary: Segfault for GridFS with long_as_object option
Apache crash on concurrent connections
description:
My script depends on multiple requests at the same time. Whenever this happens, MongoDB tends to crash immediatly, or rather quickly. I just upgraded from an older Apache/PHP 5.2 installation to see if this would fix it, but unfortunately, it does not.

Easy way to get the crash for me:


apache\bin\ab.exe -n 1000 -c 2 "http://127.0.0.1:8080//mongotest.php"

Where "mongotest.php" contains:


<?php

	$m = new Mongo( "localhost:27017", array("persist" => "x") ); // connect

?>

persistent connections or not do not matter. I tested both. At the MongoDB's console end, the following is displayed:

Wed Aug 04 23:54:20 MessagingPort recv() errno:0 No error 127.0.0.1:55172
Wed Aug 04 23:54:20 connection accepted from 127.0.0.1:55173 #2337
Wed Aug 04 23:54:20 end connection 127.0.0.1:55172
Wed Aug 04 23:54:20 MessagingPort recv() errno:0 No error 127.0.0.1:55173
Wed Aug 04 23:54:20 end connection 127.0.0.1:55173

(which might be a result from the crash of Apache itself)

Also,

apache\bin\ab.exe -n 1000 -c 1 "http://127.0.0.1:8080//mongotest.php"

(concurrency level 1, 1 request at the same time) does not make Apache crash.

I'm pretty clueless. I've tried to search a lot, but could not find anything. Maybe it is just my setup, since the operation performed is so trivial.

If you need some more info I can try to provide it to you :).

summary: unable to connect to replica set after upgrading from 1.0.11 to higher version
description:

summary: Cursors don't get closed after limit() is exhausted
description: When the cursor hits the limit, the driver doesn't close the cursor. Instead, the cursor is closed at the end of the request only.

summary: The driver crashes when there are more than FD_SETSIZE open file descriptors.
description: When the process has already more than FD_SETSIZE file descriptors open, the driver crashes when running do_timeout because the space allocated for the fd_set is not enough. Now we simply bail out, and the next version of the driver will use poll() instead (as part of the new connection handling code).

summary: findOne is slower with an index created
description:
it seems as if findOne in PHP isn't using indexes properly

to reproduce, try this:

run attached insert.php
- inserts 2.500.000 objects in objects.users with 5 possible values in the 'pet' key

run find.php
- removes all indexes
- waits 10 seconds to make sure HDD settles down
- runs findOne( { 'pet': "#RANDOM_PET#" } ) 500.000 times
- runs find( { 'pet': "#RANDOM_PET#" } ) + getNext() 500.000 times

- adds an index on 'pet' field
- waits 10 seconds to make sure HDD settles down
- runs findOne( { 'pet': "#RANDOM_PET#" } ) 500.000 times
- runs find( { 'pet': "#RANDOM_PET#" } ) + getNext() 500.000 times

summary: Support setting query flags (opts)
description: You should be able to set any option for the query/cursor as defined in the wire protocol. There should be a generic add/removeOption (maybe flag since option is already used) so you can add things like partial for sharded queries.

summary: Arbiter in the seed list acts wonky
description: See ticket CS-3284

summary: bson_decode/bson_encode not store milliseconds for MongoDate (usec)
Endless loop on non-existing file
description:
<?php
$m = new Mongo("mongodb://tmp:tmp@primary:27001/phpunit", array("replicaSet" => "foobar"));
$db = $m->selectDB("phpunit");
$c = $db->selectCollection("example");
$c->setSlaveOkay(true);

$n = new MongoGridFs($db);
$b = new MongoGridFsFile($n, array("bar.txt", "length" => 42, "_id" => new MongoId("asdfasdf")));

$b->getBytes();

?>
That will create all sorts of issues, from endless recursions to memory leaks to quick segfaults.

summary: getPID() does not return expected PID when called on custom MongoID object
description: getPID() does not return expected PID when called on a custom MongoID object. See FREE-9830 for details:

summary: MongoCollection::validate(true) doesn't set the correct scan-all flag.
description:
When running MongoCollection::validate() with the argument set to true, doesn't actually make it do a full scan and shows in the returning array:

  'warning' =>
  string(79) "Some checks omitted for speed. use {full:true} option to do more thorough scan."

summary: Touch up docs for MongoCollection::ensureIndex()
Fix MongoBinData::CUSTOM hex value in documentation
description: See: http://php.net/manual/en/class.mongobindata.php

summary: Fix MongoException code for invalid regex in documentation
description:
See: http://php.net/manual/en/class.mongoexception.php

The proper code is {{9}}, not {{8}}.

summary: Seg fault on insert timeout
description:
If an insert times out, it causes a seg fault.

Here is the code I run with an artificially low timeout to test
$result = $collection->insert($object, array("safe" => true, "timeout" => 1 ));

Nothing after this line executes. PHP crashes with a seg fault. Nothing is written to the php error log, but I see this in the apache error log:

#tail -n1 /etc/httpd/logs/error_log
[Wed Feb 29 13:16:27 2012] [notice] child pid 29333 exit signal Segmentation fault (11)

summary: MongoDB::drop() doesn't warn about arguments
description:
MongoDB::drop() doesn't accept any arguments, but it doesn't complain when passing arguments to it.

This affects multiple tests in our suite as many of them attempt to pass values to this method.

summary: MongoDB::selectCollection documentation has incorrect arguments
description: Two arguments are expected, not a single name string.

summary: PHP segfaults when master changes
description:
My previous ticket (PHP-329) may be symptom of this bug.

My original problem was that when master changes, mongo/PHP driver wasn't able to automatically connect to new master as manual says it should be. I tried to figure how mongo driver works with this script - kept script running + shut down master -> this script was unable to connect to new master. But then I got segfault...

Script to reproduce:

<?php
// mongotest_cli.php
$server = 'mongodb://localhost:27018,localhost:27017';  
$config = array ('replicaSet' => 'studio');

$mongo = new \Mongo( $server, $config );
$db = $mongo->testdb;
$coll = $db->selectCollection('testcollection');

for ($x = 0; $x < 1000; $x++)
{
        $value = $x . '_' . rand (0, 1234567);
        try {
                $coll->insert(array('foo' => $value), array ('safe' => true)); 
                echo 'Inserted ' . $value . "\n";
        } catch (Exception $ex)
        {
                echo "failed to insert " . $value . " - got exception: " . get_class($ex) . ', message: ' . $ex->getMessage() . ' and code: ' . $ex->getCode() . "\n";
        }
        sleep(1);
}
?>

How this happened:

I had 3 mongod's running. At this point:

failed to insert 12_373254 - got exception: MongoCursorException, message: couldn't get socket to send on and code: 16

I shut down primary mongod (/etc/init.d/mongod stop). At this point, previous SECONDARY becomes PRIMARY.

And at this point:

failed to insert 18_210622 - got exception: MongoCursorException, message: not master and code: 10058

I started previously shut down mongod (/etc/init.d/mongod start)

Then I shut down new PRIMARY (/etc/init.d/mongod2 stop), so this happens:

failed to insert 23_1016936 - got exception: MongoCursorException, message: couldn't get socket to send on and code: 16

and when I start new PRIMARY (/etc/init.d/mongod2 start) this happens:

Segmentation fault (core dumped)

summary: segfault when connection string refers to unknown replica set member
description:
When the mongo connection strings refers to an IP/port (in replication setup) which is not actually a replica set member, we get a segfault.
Example config:
connection string: "127.0.0.1:27001,127.0.0.1:28001" and '127.0.0.1:28001' is not part of the replica set

summary: MongoDBRef::isRef() returns null in some cases it should return false
Authentication with wrong password.. prints the password!
description:
When authenticating to a database with incorrect username/password we actually print the used password in the errormessage.
That is of course not cool.

Couldn't authenticate with database admin: username [testUser], password [testP]

summary: Mongo::__construct should only throw MongoConnectionException
setSlaveOkay not supported for GridFS queries
Coverage statistics

summary: Crashes & mem leaks
MongoBinData custom type is returned as -128

I noticed this while porting the MongoBinData PHPUnit tests to phpt. The following test fails in its expectation of the {{MongoBinData::CUSTOM}} type in the query result. {{128}} is expected but the actual value is {{-128}}.

See: https://github.com/jmikola/mongo-php-driver/blob/45fe920838d5124a5c913aa78c79bb42dce79a89/tests/mongobindata-004.phpt

summary: Error codes not being passed to MongoGridFSException
PHP driver throws tons of NOTICE messages
description:
I am using PHP 5.4 together with a driver downloaded from: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/downloads

Each 2-3 page refreshes MongoDB driver throws 100 or more NOTICE messages with different contents, such as:
NOTICE: Mongo::__construct(): parsing servers
NOTICE: Mongo::__construct(): current: localhost
NOTICE: Mongo::__construct(): done parsing
NOTICE: Mongo::__construct(): localhost:27017: pool get (0x50889d8)
NOTICE: Mongo::__construct(): localhost:27017: found in pool (0x50889d8)
NOTICE: MongoCursor::getNext(): hearing something
NOTICE: MongoCursor::getNext(): saying something

Most of them are the last two (hearing + saying).

The issue started with 1.2.10 driver (before I was using 1.2.5 for PHP 5.3)

We need to emit E_DEPRECATED warnings ASAP for the things we will be breaking in the next major release.
We have a lot of "old boolean option" and stuff that needs to be removed.

f.e. the options argument for MongoCollection::insert()

summary: Mongo::connect() doesn't validate the object
description:
<?php
class m extends Mongo { function __construct() {} }
$m = new m;
$m->connect();

?>

summary: 1.2.11 doesn't build with PHP5.2
description:
E_DEPRECATED was introduced in PHP5.3.
Interestingly enough, ZEND_ACC_DEPRECATED is available in 5.2 - just throws an E_STRICT

summary: Function MongoPool::info() is deprecated message
description:
calling MongoPool::info() under 1.2.11 gives the message
Function MongoPool::info() is deprecated

1. This isn't reflected in the documentation at http://us2.php.net/manual/en/mongopool.info.php
2. What call should be used instead to retrieve the info ?

summary: Add zend_parse_parameters_none for older builds
description: zend_parse_parameters_none() isn't available in 5.2

summary: MongoBinData::__construct(): The default value for type will change to 0 in the future
description:
The MongoBinData constructor returns
PHP Deprecated:  MongoBinData::__construct(): The default value for type will change to 0 in the future. Please pass in '0' explicitly.

Currently, the default value for type is 2. So shouldn't we pass in '2' for the parameter to obtain the same behaviour as currently ?

summary: Undeprecate *slaveOkay()
description:
As of 1.2.11 we started throwing E_DEPRECATED for

* MongoCollection::getSlaveOkay()
* MongoCollection::setSlaveOkay()
* MongoDB::getSlaveOkay()
* MongoDB::setSlaveOkay()
* Mongo::getSlaveOkay()
* Mongo::setSlaveOkay()

That is very unfortunate as the user has no alternative and is forced to suppress the error message, making the user very unhappy.

The should be undeprecated in 1.2.12 and until there exists a better alternative.

description:

The five configuration files are absolutely the same as shown below. (I just included the differences from the original file included in 10gen RPM packages.)

80a82,84

> replSet = ltset1

summary: GridFS safe option overwritten
description:
<?php
$m = new Mongo;
$db = $m->selectDB("phpunit");
$gridfs = $db->getGridFS();

$safe = array("safe" => 2);
$ok = $gridfs->storeFile(__FILE__, array( "_id" => "slaveOkayFile-file"), $safe);
?>

storeFile() will overwrite safe to = 1, while otherwise it would have wound up as
safe=1 w=2 during the MongoCollection::insert()

summary: MongoDB::selectCollection() causes Segmentation fault
MongGridFS::storeUpload() breaks on HTML5 multiple file upload
description:
when uploading multiple files from a single file input as per html5 like so:

<input type='file' name='multifiles' multiple='multiple'/>

$_FILES['multifiles']['name']  is an array of filenames instead of a single filename.

$gridFS->storeUpload('multifiles'); //throws "Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoGridFSException' with message 'tmp_name was not a string'"

Not entirely sure what the optimal behavior is to handle the scenario.  The workaround is to simply not use storeUpload, but the convenience of it is nice.  Maybe to not introduce anything backward breaking add

MongoGridFS::storeUploads($filesIndex)

the user would then be responsible for setting $_FILES['multifiles']['metadata'][imageIndex] prior to calling store.

and it would return an array of IDs index aligned to $_FILES appropriately.

Seems an edge atm, but the html5 multi-upload semantics are supported by php as well as browsers with good HTML5 support so my favorite driver should handle this :).

summary: Specifying multiple hostnames for a standalone server fails
description:
When specifying multiple hostnames/ips for the same standalone server we fail to return a valid connection.

We start by creating a connection to all of the hosts in the seedlist, and then when we are about to return a valid connection we filter the connection to attempt to find the MONGO_RP_NEAREST.
The connection_type of standalone mongo is set to 0 (implicitly), while MONGO_RP_NEAREST explicitly only finds MONGO_NODE_PRIMARY | MONGO_NODE_SECONDARY | MONGO_NODE_MONGOS.


We should probably add MONGO_NODE_STANDALONE and include that in the filter_connections() search.

See tests/generic/connection-multiple.phpt

summary: Seed list of 1 replicaset member, and one standalone, with array("replicaSet" => true) fails
Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'couldn't send query: ' when find()ing items on an empty collection
description:
A very simple test case can be found here: https://github.com/julien-c/mongo-php-daemon-bug

I'm using PHP-Daemon to periodically query a MongoDB collection using find() and then iterating over the result items, passing them to another process that removes them from the collection.

The crash happens each time an item has been removed from the collection. To reproduce it, just launch the daemon while the collection is empty, then create an item using e.g. mongo command line, and it will crash just after having removed the item.

Nothing appears in the MongoDB log.

summary: GridFS failures no longer throw MongoGridFSException
description: GridFS failure started recently to throw MongoCursorException rather then MongoGridFSException

summary: Connect to replicaset member in standalone mode
php-fpm detection and connection auto-close
description:
Problem:
Too many connections, too many open files, dead connections.  Etc.  I see these bugs and online help forum questions a lot.  FPM, spawn-fcgi, etc are becoming the new norm for php installs.  This means "connection pooling per process" is becoming the edge case, not the norm.  In our testing, any parallel php stack leaves hundreds of dead connections in it's wake, and since linux does a half-duplex shutdown on sockets it actually can look like our mongod server is attacking our app servers since it sends ACK/SYN after the app has dropped the socket.  The point is, the mongo server ends up with 900 connections or more and we only run 100 children php-fpm.

The fix:

register_shutdown_function([$mongoInstance, 'close']);


If we manually force connection closes our active connection count (and subsequent loads on all servers) drops.  And at any given time we're down to a reasonable 20-30 active connections instead of 900.  And no firewall warnings that the db server is sending stateless errant ack/syn to the app server.

The request:
Until mongo driver can be written in a way that shares connection pools across fpm/fcgi children it should do some SAPI detection and automatically disable connection pooling.

I've seen requests for Mongo::__construct(), MongoPool::configure(), or ini_set() options to disable pooling, but why force user intervention? Detect if the SAPI is FCGI/CGI/FPM and disable.  I believe this is universal since I can't imagine a use-case where you'd run a fcgi/fpm with a single child and no max_requests.

summary: is_master() crashes for standalone servers
Build error on FreeBSD (missing sys/socket.h)
description:
While building mongo-1.3.0beta2 on FreeBSD  (phpize && ./configure && make)

/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c: In function 'mongo_util_connect__sockaddr':
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:47: error: 'AF_UNIX' undeclared (first use in this function)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:47: error: (Each undeclared identifier is reported only once
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:47: error: for each function it appears in.)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:56: error: 'AF_INET' undeclared (first use in this function)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c: In function 'mongo_connection_connect':
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:124: error: 'AF_UNIX' undeclared (first use in this function)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:128: error: 'AF_INET' undeclared (first use in this function)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:134: error: 'SOCK_STREAM' undeclared (first use in this function)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:150: error: 'SOL_SOCKET' undeclared (first use in this function)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:150: error: 'SO_KEEPALIVE' undeclared (first use in this function)
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c: In function 'mongo_connection_destroy':
/root/mongo-1.3.0beta2/mcon/connections.c:273: error: 'SHUT_RDWR' undeclared (first use in this function)
*** Error code 1

This can be solved by including <sys/socket.h>


--- mcon/connections.c.orig     2012-09-18 15:43:06.000000000 +0200
+++ mcon/connections.c  2012-09-18 15:45:55.000000000 +0200
@@ -20,6 +20,7 @@
 #include <fcntl.h>
 #include <netdb.h>
 #include <sys/un.h>
+#include <sys/socket.h>
 #endif
 
 #include <unistd.h>

This patch won't break build under other unices.

summary: Connection pool not paying attention to authentication when using replicaSet=true
description:
When connecting to a replicaSet using replicaSet=true, the connection pool does not pay attention to the validity of the connection and reuses invalid connections or unauthenticated connections.

Steps to reproduce: 
1) Make a few valid connections to a replicaSet using the replicaSet=true connection parameter.  This will fill the connection pool with valid connections.
2) Modify the password in your connection string to be invalid.
3) Attempt to connect again.  Even though you have the wrong password, you can jump on an old persistent connection and still use it.

This obviously has security consequences.  It is possible for an unprivileged user to access the database even if they do not have the password because they can reuse a persistent connection from the pool.

This has other negative consequences.  If a connection fails for some reason, that invalid connection is still in the pool and will give errors to anyone that uses it.

summary: Group doesn't work correctly with initial integers and mongo.native_long = 1 in 64bit environments
description:
If you have mongo.native_long set to 1 in 64bit environment, group will fail in reduce because initial value is not number but object and all values are concatenaed into string and not added together:

["count"]=> string(1003) "[object
                Object]2874742968653174343283283040123398253734093635923323133340483295743339733494073112782785112259601970261727

Passing  $initial = array('count' => new MongoInt64(0)); doesn't seem to help and the end result is the same.

This can be worked around by passing $initial = array('count' => 0.0); but that prevents us from using 64bit integers and falling back to floating point values.

In 32bit environment, this seems to work correctly at least with 1.0.8 and 1.0.10 drivers.

summary: Mention MongoPool::setSize makes connection creation block if no connections available
MongoPool not working as expected when using php-fpm / cgi - a pool seems to be created per each child process
description:
While trying to predictably reproduce problems similar to issue PHP-202 I came across this:

MongoPool::info() reports only 1-2 used connections:

[192.168.100.52:27017....5164] => Array
	(
		[in use] => 2
		[in pool] => 0
		[remaining] => 148
		[total] => 150
		[timeout] => 5000
		[waiting] => 0
	)

[192.168.100.35:27017....5164] => Array
	(
		[in use] => 1
		[in pool] => 1
		[remaining] => 148
		[total] => 150
		[timeout] => 5000
		[waiting] => 0
	)

[192.168.100.19:27017....5164] => Array
	(
		[in use] => 1
		[in pool] => 1
		[remaining] => 148
		[total] => 150
		[timeout] => 5000
		[waiting] => 0
	)

[192.168.100.39:27017....5164] => Array
	(
		[in use] => 0
		[in pool] => 1
		[remaining] => 149
		[total] => 150
		[timeout] => 2000
		[waiting] => 0
	)


while on the actual server running php I see a lot more open connections (which shouldn't be happening in the first place):

> netstat -na | grep 27017 | grep ESTABL | awk '{print $5}' | sort | uniq -c

    294 192.168.100.19:27017
    105 192.168.100.35:27017
     50 192.168.100.39:27017
    101 192.168.100.52:27017

> netstat -na | grep 27017 | tail

tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:35765    192.168.100.19:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:36798    192.168.100.19:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:36642    192.168.100.19:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:35884    192.168.100.19:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:32908    192.168.100.52:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:52508    192.168.100.35:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:54889    192.168.100.35:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:36541    192.168.100.19:27017    ESTABLISHED
tcp        0      0 192.168.100.21:40160    192.168.100.39:27017    ESTABLISHED

As you can see from PHP output, we have limited the connection count in the pool to 150, but according to MongoPool::info() only 1-2 seem to be used. Since we are running 50 php-fpm instances my only explanation is that the pool is limited per child process. Which would make it quite ineffective - the actual limit would be 7500 cons. Or that is how it is supposed to work? Also the actual connection count reported by netstat would mean that some child's should have more than a few connections, but I ran MongoPool::info() many, many times and did not hit a child with more than 1 to .100.19 (which has most conns according to netstat). It could be bad luck though.

Or have I misunderstood completely how MongoPool works and the situation described above is expected? Would using a 5 connection limit in MongoPool be reasonable?

summary: Stale connection in connection pool after restarting MongoDB server
description:
1. Write any php script interacting with a local db (loopback or unix sock) - did not test on remote
2. Call it several times through apache to fill the connections pool
3. Restart mongo server (don't restart apache)
4. Call the script again

MongoCursorException with a code 14 will occur on the old connections from the pool. New connections (after mongo server restart) are not affected.

summary: PHP Mongo Connection Scalability
description:
PHP Mongo Connection Scalability Problem:

We run php/apache in prefork mode. Each php process has its own connection pool to a mongo server. If a server is running 200 processes and each process uses 5 connections, each physical host can generate 1000 connections to a mongo server. Multiply that by 20+ servers and it's not too difficult to generate 20k connections to a mongo server, which tests the limits of scalability. This problem is specific to forked web apps, like TP's PHP. Java usually uses one process per host so doesn't generally have this problem

summary: MongoCollection::update doc should have a note about bug #PHP-50
description:
Documentation of MongoCollection::update (http://www.php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.update.php) should contain an information about this bug: https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/PHP-50 - that _id is not saved when making an upsert. Bug was closed in 2009 as "Won't fix" and there's no information about this behaviour in the documentation. 

"Upsert" option is described as:
"If no document matches $criteria, a new document will be created from $criteria and $new_object"
which suggests that ALL the fields from $criteria and $new_object will be preserved, which is not true, because of this bug. There should be a note that _id field will ONLY be preserved if it's present in the $new_object.

summary: Re-implement ->close()
Callback map for destroying cursors
description:
When a connection fails the cursor doesn't actually know about it, and will be pointing to already free()d memory.

We need to implement some sort of callback map, registered on the connection, to be able to cleanup cursors before they attempt to use the connection again.

summary: Create new exception to be thrown for runtime errors in command helpers
description:
For reasons discussed, it would be undesirable to trigger PHP warnings/errors for errors evident in command result documents (i.e. {{ok}} is false and {{errmsg}} is populated).

I suggest creating a new exception class (MongoCommandException, MongoResultException, etc.) to be thrown when there was an error issuing a command. Although we can use the error message and code in the basic exception properties, it would be helpful to store the full Mongo result document, which may contain additional stats or information. There should be an additional property (and getter method) on the new exception class to hold that.

summary: Support w, and wtimeout in connection string
Make the w property support string
description: The 'w' property of MongoDB/MongoCollection gets forced to long, making it impossible to use 'majority' or tag names.

summary: Return full result document from command wrappers
description:
In lieu of having special return values for errors (e.g. {{false}}) and reference parameters for error messages, return the full result document from command helpers. At this time, we won't trigger warnings/errors or throw exceptions for errors. Users will need to check the "ok" result field.

I believe the following wrappers are all we have for now (I'm not including administrative things, like index management):

{{group()}}: returns the full result document already. No change needed.

{{aggregate()}}: currently unwraps the result document or boolean {{false}} on error with a warning sometimes. This needs to return the full document and not trigger warnings.

{{distinct()}}: returns an array of distinct values. We won't change this, but that's OK since it's actually very difficult to coerce an error from this command.

{{findAndModify()}}: current implementation juggles the return value between the found document (on success) or boolean {{false}}. This needs to throw an exception.

summary: Ability to choose which slaves to (not) read from
description:
The latest 1.1.0 release includes the ability for the driver to read from slaves. By default it will randomly pick any of the members in a replica set.

There are some instances where we would want to exclude certain members e.g. if the member is used for disaster recovery only and is in a different data centre or if the member has a syncdelay.

Therefore it would be useful to be able to specify members to exclude. By default it would include all, but with the option to provide a blacklist (rather than a whitelist since that would require changes every time we added a server).

summary: Incorrect method declaration
description:
Inheriting class MongoCollection couses an error:
Strict standards: Declaration of SdfDb_AbstractionMongoCollection::batchInsert() should be compatible with that of MongoCollection::batchInsert()

Let`s take a look to method declaration in documenation http://php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.batchinsert.php :
public mixed MongoCollection::batchInsert ( array $a [, array $options = array() ] )

If we use a simple Reflection to check actual declaration:
$reflection = new ReflectionClass( 'MongoCollection' );
foreach ( $reflection->getMethod('batchInsert')->getParameters() as $param ) {
	var_dump( $param->getName(), $param->isArray(), $param->isOptional() );
}

We`ll see:

string 'array_of_documents' (length=18)
boolean false
boolean false
string 'array_of_options' (length=16)
boolean false
boolean true

But correct output is:

string 'array_of_documents' (length=18)
boolean true
boolean false
string 'array_of_options' (length=16)
boolean true
boolean true

summary: Change in behaviour for return value of unsafe writes
description:
Meta ticket for https://bugs.php.net/bug.php?id=63050

Description:
------------
---
From manual page: http://www.php.net/mongo.writes
---

In version 1.2.x of the driver, when performing a write operation with the "safe 
=> false" (unsafe) flag set, it would return a boolean value.
In 1.3.0 (beta2) it appears that this is no longer the case, as these operations 
have no return value (null).
While this makes sense - since we won't really know if the operation succeeded - 
this will break applications which expect that boolean return value.

Therefore this change in behaviour should be noted on the documentation page for 
mongo write operations.

summary: Mongo PHP driver v1.3.1/1.3.2RC1 fail to connect to mongoDB 1.6.3
description:
When attempting to connect using the 1.3.x PHP driver (tested 1.3.1 on Linux and 1.3.2RC1 on Windows) to a mongoDB v1.6.3 server, an exception is thrown:

Uncaught exception 'MongoException' with message 'size of BSON doc is 78 bytes, max is 0'

We have a large number of customers using a variety of different mongoDB versions, if it's possible to fix 1.3.x so that it supports both mongoDB 1.6.x and 2.x that would be great!

summary: The secondaryPreferred read preference always considers the primary node too.
description: When using a secondaryPreferred read preference, the algorithm would always consider the primary node as part of the set to pick a random connection from. Instead, it should just ignore the last item in the list (as they're sorted with secondary first, and primary last, and there is only one primary max) and select a random node from the items left over.

summary: MongoLog::setModule/setLevel don't reset for each request
Driver spams removed RS member with connections
description: The attached {{rs_remove.php}} script connects to a three-member RS and attempts to step down and remove the primary until only a single member remains. This works fine in the 1.3.1 driver, but with 1.3.2, I'm seeing thousands of open/closed connections appear in the log of the recently removed primary. That log is also attached, with my own annotation enclosed in {{#}} marks.

summary: Undeprecate *slaveOkay() 1.3.RC1 throws error
description:
As of 1.2.11 we started throwing E_DEPRECATED for

* MongoCollection::getSlaveOkay()
* MongoCollection::setSlaveOkay()
* MongoDB::getSlaveOkay()
* MongoDB::setSlaveOkay()
* Mongo::getSlaveOkay()
* Mongo::setSlaveOkay()

That is very unfortunate as the user has no alternative and is forced to suppress the error message, making the user very unhappy.

The should be undeprecated in 1.2.12 and until there exists a better alternative.
