summary: Use real error codes for MongoConnectionException on ctor failure
Update PHP documentation regarding the "n" value that is returned on write acknowledged operations
Insufficient documentation about the driver-generated document _id passing technique
description: Mongo PHP driver uses a very unusual technique for passing driver-generated document's _id back to caller. It adds an _id element to the document array received _by value_. This is possible due to the PHP copy on write mechanism: PHP passes the original array (not its copy) to methods like MongoCollection::save(), so Mongo driver just modifies the original array. This technique is efficient but very fragile in case you wrap MongoCollection methods with some additional functionality. There are several simple PHP constructions which will trigger input array copy and thus prevent _id from being passed to the caller. The attached program demonstrates some of them. So the driver documentation should clearly specify every point where this technique is used. I've found _id passing in batchInsert(), insert() and save() - maybe there are more such points? All these points should refer to some single place (maybe part of the Tutorial section?) where this passing technique is described - including warning that one must avoid input array copying when passing document argument to the Mongocollection methods which store _id into it.

summary: Read preference get/set methods are not consistent
isMaster defaults non-master, non-arbiter connections to secondary
MongoCursor::slaveOkay() has no effect in 1.3.2
Add host, port and node type to the MongoCursor::info() return value.
description: MongoCursor::info() used to return just the server name instead of the full hash. Just the hostname is no longer part of the return value. We should add hostname back, as well as add the por tand the type of connection (PRIMARY/SECONDARY) as elements in the return value.

summary: Implement MongoCursor::setReadPreference()
description:
Right now, there is no such method on the MongoCursor class.
When this is being changed, it is also necessary to change the logic for picking a server from MongoCursor::__construct() back to MongoCursor::doQuery / mongo_do_query.

summary: MongoConnectionException when Mongo server restarted
description:
In 1.3.2RC (also verified in 1.3.1 and 1.3.0), when the server is restarted, the driver doesn't reallocate connexion pool and issues an exception instead of attemping a retry on stale socket.

It's easy to reproduce. Open a few connexions by reloading a single page a few times, restart mongo, reload the page and something like this is thrown :
{noformat} 
object(MongoConnectionException)#2 (7) {
  ["message":protected]=>
  string(100) "Failed to connect to: localhost:27017: send_package: error reading from socket: The socket is closed"
  ["string":"Exception":private]=>
  string(0) ""
  ["code":protected]=>
  int(71)
  ["file":protected]=>
  string(22) "/var/www/script.php"
  ["line":protected]=>
  int(8)
  ["trace":"Exception":private]=>
  array(1) {
    [0]=>
    array(6) {
      ["file"]=>
      string(22) "/var/www/script.php"
      ["line"]=>
      int(8)
      ["function"]=>
      string(11) "__construct"
      ["class"]=>
      string(5) "Mongo"
      ["type"]=>
      string(2) "->"
      ["args"]=>
      array(0) {
      }
    }
  }
  ["previous":"Exception":private]=>
  NULL
}

{noformat}

Which would appear to be originating in mongo_io_recv_header in io.c.

This disapears once apache is restart.



Thanks for looking into this.

summary: Driver will happily allocate memory beyond document size limits
description:
The driver currently allocates memory according to the BSON document size without any apparent validation. If the BSON response was corrupted (e.g. network instability), the driver may allocate large amounts of memory. This differs from behavior of the JS shell client, which seems to emit an error if the document size exceeds a known limit.

Perhaps this behavior is deliberate to allow for forward-compatibility if future Mongo versions increase their document size (as was the case going from 4 to 16MB).

summary: MongoDB 1.8.5 and PHP Driver 1.2.12 - Extreme Performance Issues
description:
Hi,

For the last 3 weeks we have noticed an extreme drop in performance in our PHP application. We use MongoDB 1.8.5 and the PHP driver 1.2.12.

We have not changed anything in our infrastructure, nor application code. Response times have either tripled or quadrupled in most use cases. 

We have performed tests that have eliminated the possibility that it could be our network or hardware.

Are there any other users experiencing a problem using MongoDB 1.8.5 with PHP Driver 1.2.12, or if there is a solution to this please let me know. I have searched the JIRA but could not find an issue like this.

IF YOU NEED MORE INFORMATION PLEASE LET ME KNOW. I AM MORE THAN HAPPY TO PROVIDE.

Thank you,

Chris Gibson

summary: Follow the changing replica-set status more quickly.
description:
https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/130


I add the force ping flags to any ??ping functions to follow changing replica-set status more quickly.
It'll work as the following types of the list.

[ Normal time ] 
 - No change in this patch

[ Master down ]
 1. It'll can be detected by mongo_util_link_failed(link.c) at the first requst but not important.
 2. It'll be detected by mongo_util_link_get_socket (link.c) at every request.
 3. It call mongo_util_rs_ping() with MONGO_INTERVAL_FOECE_LV1 is added by this patch.
 4. And, it call mongo_util_rs_refresh() and mongo_util_server_ismaster() as for every 5 seconds. 
    It depends on MONGO_PING_INTERVAL value.

[ Master change ]
 1. It'll be detected by mongo_util_link_failed(link.c) at the first requst as the "not master" error.
 2. It call mongo_util_rs_ping() with MONGO_INTERVAL_FOECE_LV2 is added by this patch.
 3. And, it certainly call mongo_util_rs_refresh() and mongo_util_server_ismaster() to reflesh replica-set status.
 4. Then, it'll get the status of no master.
 5. It'll do same as the [ Master down ]

summary: GridFS::storeFile with a string argument for 2Gb files
Collection level write concern overwritten by deprecated "safe" option
GridFS should only do one GLE
description:
There is no need for GridFS to do GLE on every single chunk insert.
It should only do it for the last chunk to verify everything to inserted properly.

summary: Remove redundant MongoGridFSCursor::key() implementation
description: Came across this while working on PHP-659 but it should really be fixed in its own PR. The current implementation is actually inferior to the MongoCursor method it overrides, as that ensures the current pointer is valid before accessing it.

summary: PHP Mongo's driver has no effects on Mongo Cursor timeout
Driver uses replication timeout instead of connection timeout while reconnecting to server
PHP 1.3.0dev does not honor connection timeout
Add support for the connectTimeoutMS connection parameter
description: We currently support a "timeout" parameter, but the correct time of it is connectTimeoutMS according to the spec.

summary: sort out timeout vs wtimeout mess
description:
The code seems to be mixing wtimeout (only applicable to WriteConcern timeouts) and timeouts (i.e. socket timeout).

These are two completely unrelated options and should not be inheriting eachother.

For unknown reason the wtimeout is also set to PHP_MONGO_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT - which is explicitly wrong. A wtimeout should _NOT_ default to anything as w=42 should wait indefinitely.

The documentations also confuse these options.

summary: Forking and connection handling still fails
description: tests/generic/connection-fork.phpt worked for a while, but starting failing. Check with git bisect when it failed.

summary: setReadPreference() should allow empty $tags array for primary mode
description:
Currently, the driver emits a warning if anything is passed as the {{$tags}} parameter for a {{primary}} read preference mode. An empty {{$tags}} array should be permitted, since it really specifies no tags at all.

When proxying this method in PHP code, the logical type-hint for {{$tags}} is {{array $tags = array()}}, unless I'm mistaken. Without this fix, proxying code will be forced to conditionally specify or not specify the second {{setReadPreference()}} argument.

summary: setReadPreference() should clear out existing tags if none are provided
description: It's possible to set tags on a non-primary read preference mode and then call {{setReadPreference(MongoClient::RP_PRIMARY)}} and have tag sets remain in place. They should be cleared out if no provided during a call to {{setReadPreference()}}.

summary: setReadPreference() should not modify read preferences if an error occurs
description:
As-is, {{php_mongo_set_readpreference()}} may leave the read preference in an inconsistent state if an error is encountered while processing tagsets. If an error is encountered (either an invalid mode string or bad tags), the underlying read preference should not be changed.

This poses a slight BC change if users were passing invalid tagsets earlier but still relied on the mode being changed.

summary: Remove misplaced MongoId in getIndexInfo() expected ouput docs
description: See: http://php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.getindexinfo.php

summary: MongoDate usec not properly decoded on 32-bit platform
description:
See these failing tests:

 * https://travis-ci.org/doctrine/mongodb-odm/jobs/4455390
 * https://github.com/doctrine/mongodb-odm/blob/7a138482bf99cb01bd1eb0ff42441215172add67/tests/Doctrine/ODM/MongoDB/Tests/Functional/RawTypeTest.php

summary: PHP 1.2/1.3 Driver does not honour connection 'timeout'
Default connect() timeout is 1000 _seconds_, not milliseconds
description:
After we introduced the connectTimeoutMS option the default connect timeout changed from 1 second to 1000 seconds when connectTimeoutMS is = 0.

This is because the default value passed to the function used to be 1000 _ms_ but the logic inside the function checked if there was a timeout - if not, fell back to 1000 _ms_. This else condition never kicked in as the caller had the value hardcoded.

summary: Percentage symbol is not escaped in error message about duplicate entries
mongo_read_preference_dtor() will segfault if invoked twice
description: {{mongo_read_preference_dtor()}} should check if {{tagset}} is null before dereferencing it. This is possible to trigger via PHP if {{setReadPreference()}} is called with a tag set of {{array(1)}}, as it will trigger the warning in {{php_mongo_use_tagsets()}} and not add any tag sets to the {{mongo_read_preference}} object. {{mongo_read_preference_dtor()}} was already called once in that function to clear out the previous tag set values, and when PHP naturally deconstructs the extension data structures, it will segfault on the final call to {{mongo_read_preference_dtor()}}.

summary: "nolock" option in MongoDB::execute method
description: Since using execute is, at the moment, the only way to update a collection using expressions or references (e.g. field => field * 2), I think is very important to allow users to use the option "nolock", available in mongo since 1.7.2, using the execute db command.

summary: Random "unauthorized db" messages
description:
I have random issues on running findOne with the following environnement:
- mongo 2.0.2 + replica set + keyFile
- PHP client from http://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/zipball/v1.2/ or git branch master
- apache2 MPM with multiple sites (multiple users AND multiple replica sets). A single PHP request will only connect to one rs with one user at a time, but a future request may require to connect to a different rs with a different user.

The message is something like this:
{quote}[Tue Jan 31 09:41:47 2012] [error] [client xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx] PHP Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoCursorException' with message 'unauthorized db:xxxx lock type:-1 client:.... in xxxxxxxx\nStack trace:\n#0 xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx: MongoCollection ->findOne(Array)\n#1{quote}

I don't have the issue with version 1.2.6 of the driver.
I could not test with 1.2.7 because of #PHP-311

summary: Incorrect failure checks for stream reads
description:
Coverity flagged this for a memory leak.. which probably is worse then it sounds.

recv can only return 0 (on error or no data) but its return value is being checked against -1 which can never be true.

https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/master/cursor.c#L204

summary: MongoId should not get constructed when passing in an invalid ID.
description:
You should not be able to construct a new instance of \MongoId with an invalid identifier.  Currently, you are able to and it leads to inconsistencies as well as too many invalid queries.

new \MongoId('i am not a valid id!') -> '509403bd3689a3f942000000' (a valid mongo id)

Currently, before running all queries, we validate MongoId's, but I feel that this is a hack:

if ($id !== (new \MongoId($id)->__toString()) throw new InvalidMongoIdException();

Now we can track when users are passing in invalid ids and stop from unnecessary DB look-ups.

I propose that a null argument is what creates a new MongoId, and when given an argument, it wraps the given ID in a MongoId object, and when invalid, an exception is thrown.

Cheers!

summary: Update limits according to default (16MB) or server info (maxMessageSizeBytes)
description: Investigate whether our 16000000 insert limit shouldn't really be 16MB instead.

summary: Add support for Auth Specification
Constant attempt to reconnect to a down server takes too long
description:
When a member of a replicaset is down we will constantly attempt to connect to it on every 'new MongoClient'.
This is because when the connection fails we deregister it from our connection registry and drop it completely. On the next 'new MongoClient' call we execute a topology discovery and see a server we don't have a connection to and will attempt to create a connection to it as we thought it was a new replicaset member.

When the server hosting that server is powered off this creates major problems as by default we will wait for a second until deciding to timeout. This will happen on every single 'new MongoClient' call, effectively making all requests to the application lag by 1secon

summary: When using replica sets and setSlaveOkay queries slow if a secondary is down...
description:
I've been testing out geo-distributed replica sets with apache2 and
the php driver. There's a few weird things about how the driver works
(like *never* querying a primary if setSlaveOkay is set) but they
mostly can be worked around.

My problem arises when a replica set secondary goes down. Currently I
have a primary and secondary in the us-east1a AZ, and one secondary in
the eu-west1b AZ. Now if the PHP driver on the us-east1a web servers
sees *all* the members of the replica set then everything's fast and ok, but if
I take down the eu-west1b replica set member the php driver queries
slow down by several orders of magnitude (i.e. from 4ms to 400ms+). It
seems that, even though the driver has a very low-latency connection
to a primary and secondary in the same AZ, queries still get slowed
down because it cannot reach the eu-west1b secondary.

is_master() failing with mongodb <= 1.8.3
description:
This bug involves the use of older mongodb instances. I could not find which versions of mongodb are supported by which driver versions. If 1.8.3 is too old to be supported, this bug should be ignored.

Bug PHP-489 solved the issue of MongoClient seg faulting when the "me" key isn't returned by the server when using the 'replicaSet' option (In the bugs case, it was when 'replicaSet' was used in standalone mode). The "me" property was added on August 19th, 2011, and is missing from mongodb versions 1.8.3 or lower ([https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/blame/master/src/mongo/db/repl/rs.cpp#L326]). This results in 'is_master' failing even though mongodb 1.8.3 is a master in a replica set. 

If the problem cannot be solved, it would at least be good to have warning and error messages that represent the conditions which failed and what the problem might be. 

The warning message thrown by the code now,
bq. Host does not seem to be a replicaset member
([https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/master/mcon/connections.c#L471]) is correct in the case of a standalone server, but not if its a mongodb version problem. 

Better would be: 
bq.'me' not present in db.isMaster(). Either host is not a replicaset member or using old version of mongodb

Furthermore, without adjusting the MongoLog levels, trying to connect to a replicaset with the above condition results in
bq. PHP Notice: Could not connect to MongoDB.  Is the "mongo" process running? No candidate servers found
Which is also not helpful or correct.

summary: MongoCollection::find sometimes wants parameter 1 to be MongoClient
description:
This is a really bizarre error that appears to have been introduced in 1.3.2. Under very specific conditions, I consistently get this warning:

Warning: MongoCollection::find() expects parameter 1 to be MongoClient, null given

which results in an improperly constructed cursor. Attempting to inspect the object or the arguments eliminates the error.

MongoCollection is subclassed with a find() method that does some utility stuff before invoking parent's find() as call_user_func_array(array(parent,'find'),func_get_args())

summary: Queries/commands to mongos should inherit read preferences from MongoDB and MongoCollection objects
description:
{{tests/generic/bug00535.phpt}} from [PR #331|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/331] only tested log output for verifying read preference support in command helpers. These helpers only seem to inherit read preferences from MongoClient. Command helpers on MongoDB should inherit from that class, and those on MongoCollection (e.g. {{aggregate()}} and {{count()}}) should inherit from their own class as well.

For testing this on the 1.3.x branch, we may be able to check for the executed command in the {{system.profile}} collection.

Have Secondary Reads take Optime into consideration
description:
As it stands we use nearest to determine which secondary we should use for queries when using a secondary read preference.

Can we add optime as a metric for determining which would be suitable to use? As an example we could use a node which has just returned from maintenance and is not fully caught up yet.

summary: Connection method not selecting nearest secondary or ignoring down servers
description:
Here's the setup - 6 servers (1 master, 5 slaves) are located in the main DC.  Two additional slaves are located 120ms away in the remote DC.

Without the remote servers added to the set, our site has a 30ms response time.

With them added to the set, but DOWN (ie. mongodb is stopped on the remote machines), response time is 90ms due to connection issues.

With them added and UP, response time is 90ms, but one call out of eight will be 8000ms, indicating to me that the local server is connecting to the remote secondary.

Link to graphs showing the issue: http://imgur.com/a/nkScy

summary: Cursor problem with PHP driver 1.3.4 and mongodb 2.2.3 (replicaset)
Write operations apply ReadPreferenceTags when finding primary
description: When setting ReadPreferenceTags, they must include (atleast a fallback) tags matching the primary, otherwise the candidate selection logic will discard of the primary - and no inserts or commands are allowed to be executed.

summary: MongoClient connection fails if RP tags match nothing

I would not expect to see that exception until I actually issued a query.

summary: When a secondary goes into recovery mode, we should disconnect from it
description:

Currently we don't do anything on this error and will continue using the socket and fail everytime until we hit the ismaster interval

summary: Support the oplog_replay query flag
description:
This flag is used for efficient traversal of oplog/capped-collection queries of this form:

{code}{ts : { $gt : <last-ts> }}{code}

When set the server will do something closer to a binary search to find the correct extent and document without an index in the capped collection.

Docs should reflect this as well. http://us3.php.net/manual/en/mongocursor.setflag.php

summary: Passing in invalid MongoDB to MongoDBRef::get() segfaults
Improve numeric check for write concern option
description:
See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/363#issuecomment-16239584

Checking the first character for 0-9 or a hyphen is not sufficient. Also, we should use {{strtol()}} instead of {{atoi()}} in order to allow for error checking.

summary: MongoCursor ctor doesn't validate the MongoClient object
IS_SCALAR_*() doesn't account for resources
description:
We use IS_SCALAR_P() in several places in the source code to do input validation.

This works fine fine expecting it to return true on scalar, but when used as !IS_SCALAR_P() to verify it can be used with array/objects it will return !false for resources, hence leading to all sorts of bad things happening all over.

summary: Rewrite to_index_string to use smart_str and a real C function
description: to_index_string uses tricky allocation strategies and should be rewritten to use smart_str. The function also needs to return the length as a by-ref second argument to avoid strlen()'s.

summary: findAndModify returns empty array when nothing is found
description: The findAndModify command returns array() when no rows matched the query. However this is indistinguishable from a matched row which has no fields which are in the fields (the third) argument. Returning NULL when nothing if matched would be more in line with how findOne works.

summary: MongoCollection::batchInsert() exceptions can obscure BSON encoding exceptions
description:
See: https://github.com/doctrine/mongodb-odm/issues/614

If a basic BSON encoding exception (e.g. "non-utf8 string: ...") occurs during a batch insertion, {{batchInsert()}} will end up throwing its own exception with an irrelevant message (e.g. "current batch size is too large: 193, max: 48000000"). The "batch size too large" message should be reserved for actual cases where the size exceeds the max. Encoding exceptions should be propagated.

Note: the outer exception goes capture the previous one (accessible via {{Exception::getPrevious()}}, but this can be quite confusing for users.

summary: Busy loop when a file descriptors exceed 1024
description:
Because select() syscall can only poll descriptors up to 1024, a connection with descriptor ID over 1024 will cause an endless busy loop in mcon/io.c: mongo_io_wait_with_timeout(). 

Attached is a patch to convert select() to use poll() syscall instead.

summary: Allow skipping in GridFS Files
description:
Allow skipping around in the gridfs file like this:

JAVA-332 : https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-java-driver/commit/e96482235863c8a40347e47f87424ff021af993d

summary: Max size of BSON doc is 0 errors
description:
Short:

We get lots of these errors from PHP when mongo server is congested:
'MongoException' with message 'size of BSON doc is 277 bytes, max is 0'

This error is confusing and may indicate something more insidious is happening.

Long:

PHP servers submit many small documents to rather beefy mongo server (16GB quad/HT box, *no other responsibilities*, spinning disk *may* be 'green' style low-power). The mongo database on this server is a log server -- 99.9% of the time it's just accepting inserts.

If the mongo server becomes overloaded with inserts the PHP driver will start to scream:

[Tue Apr 16 08:00:20 2013] [error] [client 172.18.0.1] PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoException' with message 'size of BSON doc is 277 bytes, max is 0' in /home/www/PHP/common.php:632\nStack trace:\n#0 /home/www/PHP/common.php(632): MongoCollection->insert(Array)\n#1 /home/www/PHP/sell.php(142): RecordAccounting('/home/www/PHP/s...', 142, Array, Array)\n#2 {main}\n  thrown in /home/www/PHP/common.php on line 632

We get reams of these depending on how bad things are on the server -- the size varies from a 100's of bytes to few KB.

We also run several other mongo databases but they are in replica sets; when these servers choke up from load we get quite different behavior (connection or cursor timeouts, errors about not being able to find a host etc). This bogus "max size is 0" error seems peculiar and I'm not at all sure that it's a problem with the PHP driver per se (it may be just that the exception message is confusing)

Here's how it's happening in our cluster:

2xPHP servers sending 50-100 inserts/second combined to the mongo server
- at this point it seems fine, no problems

Start blasting IO on mongo server (eg: untar a 70GB archive).
- PHP servers start to spew errors

Aside: we're blasting IO on this box to mongorestore a large collection; ultimately we found that running this process from a remote box to write to the db over the network balanced the load better -- it's been running for 12hrs now and has instead of a waterfall of these errors we've only had a few hundred.

summary: When connection string contains non-rs member, continues to reconnect constantly
description:
We have a long running PHP script that has piped input, that pushes it into MongoDB. It's connection string erroneously contained a server that was running, but no longer part of the replica set. This _seriously_ degraded performance. We were seeing lag of 500ms, after removing it the lag dropped down to 0.00001.

These two lines (slightly redacted) appear regularly throughout the file:
PHP Notice:  CON     WARN: Host does not seem to be a replicaset member (alpha.example.com:32783) in /etc/nagios3/scripts/upload_status.php on line 12
PHP Notice:  CON     WARN: discover_topology: ismaster return with an error for monitor-alpha.example.com:32783: [Host does not seem to be a replicaset member (alpha.example.com:32783)] in /etc/nagios3/scripts/upload_status.php on line 12

I wouldn't have expected that bad listing to affect performance so drastically.

summary: connectTimeoutMS - in micro or milli seconds?
description:
Hi,

PHP documentation seems to be not describing whether connectTimeoutMS is in micro or milliseconds.

Can anyone please clarify?

cheers,
/Marcin

summary: PHP Driver crashes Apache
Driver should prevent \0 characters in keys, collection and database names
Add the MongoClient::killCursor method to kill a cursor on the server on 64-bit platforms
ReadPreference.PrimaryPreferred + Tag sets should send to primary if available
description: When you combine a read preference of primary preferred with tag sets, the read should be routed to a primary if available and only sent to secondaries if no primary is available http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/core/read-preference/

summary: MongoDate support for 64-bit timestamps on 32-bit systems
description:
This is similar to {{DateTime::getTimestamp()}}'s limitation on 32-bit systems. Instead, users can use {{format('U')}} to return 64-bit integers as strings. I realize the {{$sec}} and {{$usec}} properties of MongoDate will remain integers, but perhaps we can support this via {{toString()}}?

Currently, ISODate("3000-01-01T12:00:00Z") shows up as "0.00000000 -1856015168" in PHP.

summary: auto gen _id clashes on heavy inserts
description: Not sure if this is related to PHP-508? but we have a collection with an average size of 65 million records (a log that uses a TTL index) and with version 1.4.0 of the php client or higher we get _id clashes now using the auto generated ids. We have an insertion rate of hundreds per second.

summary: php_mongo_dbref_create() doesn't handle MongoId values
description:
{{php_mongo_dbref_create()}} is attempting to convert MongoId values into strings, which it should not do. The document's ID should be used as-is to fill the {{$id}} field in the DBRef.

Internally, {{convert_to_string_ex()}} is being given a zval pointer, when it actually expects a zvpp. This can cause segfaults in odd places (e.g. php_mongo's GINIT hostname length calculation), depending on what memory gets clobbered.

Also, the {{createDBRef()}} helper methods on MongoDB and MongoCollection also inject the database name (i.e. {{$db}} field) into the DBRef, which is a departure from the old behavior.

summary: PHP Driver crashes on shutdown
description:
Attempting to save a document to Mongo using PHP crashes Apache (WAMP). Verified connecting to both local and remote mongodb instances.

Driver version is php_mongo-1.4.2-5.3-vc9.dll

Simple PHP sample attached.

summary: Add support for secondaryAcceptableLatencyMS
description:
Used to filter out very "slow" members when using the RP NEAREST or SECONDARY_PREFERRED.

Specifies the maximum accepted latency of a server which should be considered when using RP NEAREST or SECONDARY_PREFERRED.

Default value is 15ms.

All all secondaries have higher latency, the primary is returned.

summary: New Replica Set connections hang when secondaries are creating indexes during initialSync
No need to call ismaster 2times
description:
We do more roundtrips to the server then we really need when establishing our view of the world.
get_server_flags (aka: ismaster)
ping
ismaster

We shouldn't need to call ismaster again as the get_server_flags already retrieves the data (but only uses maxBsonObjectSize, isdbgrid, tags)..

summary: BSON decoding segfaults if string length prefix is zero
DBRef refactoring broke BC for $id parameter handling
description:
Refactoring in PHP-820 changed the behavior of MongoDBRef::create(), such that it now attempts to resolve an array/object zval passed as its {{$id}} argument to an {{_id}} field. The {{$id}} parameter should be used as-is.

The resolution behavior should only apply to the collection/db helper methods.

Also, the refactoring added PHP warnings and NULL returns when an array/object zval cannot be resolved to its {{_id}} field or when the argument is a resource type. Previously, resources were used as-is and NULL would be returned (without error) if, and only if, an array argument was missing an {{_id}} field. Objects were used as-is.

This issue will restore behavior from the 1.4.x branch. Any changes to behavior for 1.5.0 or a later release should be outlined in a separate ticket, so it can be recorded in the release log and online documentation.

summary: Cursor exception should not print strerror() as int
Fix documentation of MongoCursor::$timeout default value
description: The current docs list the default value of the static MongoCursor property as 20000 (it's 30000). Also, the timeout() method documentation says default behavior is to wait forever (it's not).

summary: Fix the persistence issue with logging stream contexts
description: Adding a stream context is per stream, and we keep the streams open even between requests. In a next request, the context should not be set anymore.

summary: Detect server state changes
description:
When a mongod changes its state without dropping connections (i.e. when forcing the server into recovery mode) we don't actually detect that change and still try to read off it.

The server does block the read, which results in an exception being thrown.
Which shouldn't have tried to read of that server though (once we noticed he change during regularly scheduled ismaster).

This only happens in the 1.4 branch, the 1.5 branch has merged the "server flags" and the "ismaster" functions, as they actually do the same thing.

summary: Segfault when unregistering broken server
description:
When a server needs to be killed off due to brokeness we need to unregister it from our known servers lists so we don't try to use it.

This only happens in master.
See: mongocollection-find_error-001.phpt

summary: BSON decoding of DBPointer does not set MongoId::$id property
BSON decoding segfaults reading past buffer endpoint
description:
This is the flip side of PHP-894. A corrupted string length could easily point outside of the buffer endpoint and segfault. The current {{INVALID_STRING_LEN}} macro uses a generic upper bound. We can be much more precise with pointer arithmetic here.

Additionally, we should be more defensive about reading other types as well and before any recursive {{bson_to_zval()}} calls (for arrays, objects, and code scopes), we should check their length first (otherwise we don't know the parent buffer's endpoint after recursing).

summary: PHP driver should use SSLv23 instead of TLS
description:
The PHP driver currently uses STREAM_CRYPTO_METHOD_TLS_CLIENT for SSL. It should switch to STREAM_CRYPTO_METHOD_SSLv23_CLIENT so that the client and server can negotiate the most secure protocol and cipher suite. SSLv23 will allow the use of TLSv1.1 and TLSv1.2 if the client and server OpenSSL versions support these protocols.

http://www.openssl.org/docs/ssl/SSL_CTX_new.html

summary: batchSize() can not be called after a cursor has been iterating contradicting documentation
description: The documentation states that ``batchSize()`` can be called after a cursor has started iterating. This does not seem to be the case.

summary: Handle server command responses without "ok" field
description: According to SERVER-10891, commands don't return "ok" field if targeting databases with invalid characters. (Essentially, the server is failing to execute a query on "$cmd", and it hasn't yet entered the command-processing path.) Drivers must expect a command response document without an "ok" field, in which case "$err" will be set.

summary: Documentation for MongoCursor::setReadPreference is unclear.
description: In documentation to MongoCursor::setReadPreference said that method returns bool, but in fact it returns MongoCursor object.

summary: Log a warning when ignoring user-specified read preference
WriteConcern failure exception should include the entire GLE document
description:
On insert failure with WriteConcern we throw an MongoCursorException, populating the exception message and errorcode.

There are however other fields in the GLE document that are of value, so the entire GLE document needs to be accessible.

Currently we have an MongoResultException which is thrown for findAndModify command failures, which includes the entire result document (slightly different from the GLE document).

We should probably extend MongoCursorException with MongoWriteConcernException class which includes the GLE document?

summary: How to use like query in codeigniter
description:
How to use mongodb like query for search functionality in codeigniter for matching more than one document in mongodb collection?
(i.e)search parameters such as firstname,lastname,username,email
if above paraneters has a keyword " s " matching all the documents in the collection.

summary: Random Uncaught exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'No candidate servers found'
description:
I'm getting this error randomly on both of my servers. I upgraded the php driver just in case, and it's still happening.

There is no election going on. The other server keeps working fine when the other fails.

Both servers are running php-fpm feeding a 3rd one running nginx to load balance. Both servers are members of the replicaset, one of them is primary. A 3rd replica member is in a different location.

On the server that's secondary:
[18-Oct-2013 11:21:33 America/Los_Angeles] PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'No candidate servers found' in mongo.php:24
Stack trace:
#0 /mongo.php(24): MongoCollection->findOne()
#1 /search.php(3): require('/...')
#2 {main}
  thrown in /mongo.php on line 24

Stack trace:
#0 /mongo.php(24): MongoCollection->findOne()
#1 /index.php(4): require('/...')
#2 {main}
  thrown in /mongo.php on line 24

As soon as the error occurs, I went on in the mongo shell and issued an rs.status() -- no election going on, everything normal. And as mentioned above, the 2nd server is still working fine. They both get the issue randomly though, and lasts a few minutes.

summary: Improve the Stream Notification API
description:
In v1.4 we added experimental logging/callback API for most events.

This API needs to be improved a bit.

The suggestion is to use the "notification" system of the PHP Streams (which the v1.4 has a POC of) for all notifications.
This way the user only has to register one callback function and can from there decide if he cares about the specific event or not.

summary: Cucumber features in the meta driver not portable to Behat for the PHP driver
description:
When converting the Cucumber tests for use with Behat to check the PHP driver, some scenarios generate regexes with named subpatterns in which the subpattern name is longer than 32 characters, which is not supported by the PCRE extension in PHP, like this one:

This happens for instance with the last scenario in deserialize.feature:
      Scenario Outline: Deserialize code
        Given an IO stream containing <hex_bytes>
        When I deserialize the stream
        Then the result should be a code value "<code>" with scope <scope>

        Examples:
        | hex_bytes                                                                          | code         | scope      |
        | 190000000d6b000d00000066756e6374696f6e28297b7d0000                                 | function(){} |            |
        | 290000000f6b00210000000d00000066756e6374696f6e28297b7d000c000000106100010000000000 | function(){} | {"a" : 1}  |

which causes this subpattern to be created:

?P<arg1d00000066756e6374696f6e28297b7d000c0000001061000100000000>

summary: Hang out when connecting to multiples mongos and one mongos host is down
description:
Hi,

We have a shard cluster and we connect to it through 3 mongos.
When a mongos (as a tcp service) comes down, the php driver correctly handles the event and all works fine, but when the host running the mongos comes down (reboot for kernel update for example), php drivers hang out for several seconds (kind of ip timeout i guess) before connecting to the cluster through another mongos

Can we avoid that timeout ?

summary: Using $inc operator in Collection->update results in incrementing double increment value
Random crash of Apache due to connection with mongo server
description:
PHP driver v1.4.4. makes Apache crash 'randomly'. I did some tests using the 'ab' command calling a very simple PHP script which simply reads or writes from/to a database. I got the following results:
- with a single client, no real problem except a connection timeout occurring after about 16350 requests sent.
- with 2 or more clients, a 'connection reset by peer (54)' error occurs quite often, causing Apache to stop.
- with 2 or more clients, the error still happens but less frequently when I add MongoClient::close() at the end of the script.

See attached file for complete bench results. I also joined the PHP script file used for testing.

summary: Memory Leak when inserting many documents
description:
Hi

I have a script which is looping though a large amount of documents (~35000) which are getting imported in mongoDB using this code:

$result = $this->productsCollection->update(
    array('productid' => $finalDocument['productid'], 'lang' => $countryCode),
     array('$set' => $finalDocument),
     array('upsert' => true)
);

If this code runs, the memory_get_peak_usage() shows an increase of ~200 KB every 3-4 loops. If I'm removing the MongoDB update from my Code the Memory consumption is a flat line. So I figure the bus must be in the Mongo Driver...

Thanks
Matthias

summary: Throw MongoDuplicateKeyException on duplicate key errors
description:
Duplicate Key error should be specifically catchable so the user can programmatically deal with it.
This is needed when for example attempting to re-insert a document after a WriteConcern failure or timeout

summary: Throw MongoConnectionException on stream failures
description:
The PHP Streams layer will emit E_WARNINGs when it fails to read/write, establish ssl connection, resolve hostname, ...

These should be converted into exceptions right away.

summary: php driver still wait for a timeout even after TCP RST packet

It's easy to reproduce by adding a non-existent server to a connection string and on the same machine do:
iptables -I OUTPUT -d <dummyIP> -j REJECT

The connector still waits for the time-out...

summary: New write operation method for insert, update, remove
Include maxWriteBatchSize and maxMessageSizeBytes in connection info
Inline mapReduce command ignores read preferences
RP support for "mapReduce" command, not just "mapreduce"

The server allows either to be used, while other forms of the string (e.g. "MapReduce") are not allowed.

summary: JSON detection
description:
JSON detection rely on HAVE_JSON

Problem: when php is build without json extension but json or jsonc extension installed from pecl, it is not detected.

Proposal, add in config.m4

{code}
AC_MSG_CHECKING(for JSON)
if test -f "$phpincludedir/ext/json/php_json.h"; then
    AC_DEFINE(HAVE_JSON, 1, [JSON support])
    AC_MSG_RESULT(Yes)
else
    AC_MSG_RESULT(Missing json or jsonc extension)
 fi
{code}

Mostly affected: all linux distro which have drop non-free json extension (debian, fedora, ubuntu, mandriva, ...)

summary: MongoCollection doesn't respect its own write concern for GLE ops
description:
In {{collection.c}}, the {{is_gle_op()}} function, which decides whether we append a GLE command to legacy write ops, only checks the write concern of the top-level link (MongoClient's server options) and the options array passed to the MongoCollection method.

Instead, it should read the MongoCollection's own write concern and trust that it was property inherited from the "parent" MongoDB and MongoClient classes.

summary: Cannot switch from majority to lower WriteConcern
MongoDB and MongoCollection do not inherit string write concerns
description: In {{mcon/parse.c}}, {{default_w}} and {{default_wstring}} are initially set to -1 and NULL before parsing, respectively. {{default_wtimeout}} is also initialized to -1. During parsing, if a {{w}} integer option is parsed, {{default_wstring}} is left as NULL and {{w}} is assigned; however, if a {{w}} string is parsed, {{default_w}} is set to {{1}} and then the string is copied to {{default_wtimeout}}. This confuses the inheritance logic in MongoDB's constructor, which will never consider {{default_wstring}} at all unless {{default_w}} is -1.

summary: MongoCollection::commandCursor should not require batchSize option to be set manually
description: commandCursor would not automatically add a command level cursor->batchSize option if one was not set in user land. This should of course work.

summary: JSON is optional at buildtime but requires at runtime
description:
Very minor issue.

JSON is optional at build time, but once used, it is required at runtime

So I think

{code}
-        ZEND_MOD_OPTIONAL("json")
+#if HAVE_JSON
+       ZEND_MOD_REQUIRED("json")
+#endif
{code}

summary: Read preferences are not respected with command cursors
please update description of 'fsync' write concern flag
description:
Since your driver's documentation (e.g. [here|http://php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.update.php]) describes the 'fsync' write concern flag, can you please update its description so that it accurately describes the current behavior, which is:

# 'fsync' is identical to 'j' if mongod is running with a journal (today, 99% of users should be here)
# 'fsync' requests that the write be sync()'d to disk only if mongod is not running with a journal
# 'fsync' cannot be used simultaneously with the 'j' flag

The point is that:
- 'j' means 'sync via the journal if present, or error if not, or if 'fsync' is also set'
- 'fsync' means 'sync via the journal if present, or sync via msync() if there is no journal, and error if 'j' is also set'

Additionally, could you please mark the 'fsync' flag as "Deprecated; do not use", following this commit [here|https://github.com/mongodb/docs/commit/75c648730b54a920cf85372fd27ab8600b67b794]

Please see SERVER-11399 for background.

Thanks!

summary: Deprecate static properties
description: MongoCursor::$timeout & MongoCursor::$slaveOkay should be overloaded and emit deprecation notices.

summary: Include connection info in findAndModify exception messages
Support maxTimeMS and exceptions in MongoCollection::group()
Support maxTimeMS option on MongoCollection::findOne()
description: This should have been implemented as part of PHP-861 (according to the spec and QA test plan), but it was overlooked.

summary: Empty Batch Insert should throw exception
description:
mongod 2.6.0-rc2 will throw an exception when sent an empty batch.

This means we cannot 'fake' the NOOP and return array("ok" => true) right away, and that change, along with tests/standalone/mongoinsertbatch-007.phpt needs to be reverted.

summary: Support $options arg in MongoCollection::aggregate()
add allowDiskUse:true to the top-level of an aggregate command
description: allowDiskUse is an optional, top-level, boolean field on the aggregate command. If no value is specified by the caller for allowDiskUse, the default behavior should be to not include the field at all in the command document.

summary: Support parallelCollectionScan command
description:
Implement {{MongoCollection::parallelCollectionScan()}}, which returns an array of command cursor objects.

If we use [python's helper|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver/commit/940d73f672227dd1d5a183ffd223fe0a428d68fc] as an example, it can take a {{$numCursors}} argument followed by an {{$options}} array. Alternatively, we could just take an {{$options}} array and require {{numCursors}} to be set.

!http://www.reactiongifs.com/wp-content/uploads/2013/07/crazy-celebration.gif!

summary: Ensure MongoCommandCursor implements Iterator interface properly
description:
Derick discovered an incompatibility with how MongoCommandCursor implements the Iterator interface while testing the cursors returned from {{parallelCollectionScan}} with PHP's [MultipleIterator|http://www.php.net/manual/en/class.multipleiterator.php] class. Ensure that the Iterator methods in MongoCommandCursor do exactly what they should.

Note: MongoCursor also deviates in strictly adhering to the Iterator interface, but we will not refactor it at this time. This issue only concerns the command cursor class.

summary: Off-by-one error in MongoCommandCursor::key() index
description: {{MongoCommandCursor::key()}} reports {{-1}} for the first result document's index. This error was hidden when the document contained an {{_id}} field.

summary: Command cursor doesn't check if cursor exists
MongoCollection::aggregateCursor batch size defaults should be: 101, 0 (server default)
description: The current defaults are 101, 101 which is unlike normal cursors, which are 101, 0 (server chosen limit)

description:
When querying hard, from a PHP script a collections this this stats:

db.collection_name.stats()

	"totalIndexSize" : 15827902048,
	"indexSizes" : {
		"_id_" : 1164483152,
               // other indexes here ...
	},
	"ok" : 1
}

I see that the PHP process exits with code 3 and after an strace I get the following segmentation fault error:

gettimeofday({1395595389, 151312}, NULL) = 0

gettimeofday({1395595389, 152367}, NULL) = 0
gettimeofday({1395595389, 152426}, NULL) = 0
sendto(8, "\356\0\0\0\274\37\0\0\0\0\0\0\324\7\0\0\0\0\0\0namespace.$cmd\0"..., 238, 0, NULL, 0) = 238
select(9, [8], NULL, [8], {30, 0})      = 0 (Timeout)
shutdown(8, 2 /* send and receive */)   = 0
close(8)                                = 0
--- SIGSEGV (Segmentation fault) @ 0 (0) ---
write(3, "Received signal 11: segmentation"..., 43) = 43
write(3, "fatal signal (SIGSEGV, SIGFPE, S"..., 136) = 136
write(3, "No backtrace on this platform.\n", 31) = 31
exit_group(3)                           = ?

But the interesting thing is that I run the script again 2 or 3 times, and each time it goes a step further until I am able to close the task.

It seems that the Segmentation Fault is caused by the working set that it is not loaded. Using mongostat infact I can see 700 page faults per second.

So when the collection is not warmed up and I perform distinct and find on it I get this wrong behaviour.

summary: Read timed out after reading 0 bytes, waited for 0.000000 seconds
description:
After upgrading from mongo 2.2.4 / PHP driver 1.3.7 to versions 2.4.6 / 1.4.4, we started seeing this connection error intermittently when running a find query on a sharded replicaset (mongos running locally).  We've been able to reproduce it with drivers 1.4.2 and 1.4.0.  When reverting to driver 1.3.7, the error disappears.  So it appears to be something introduced in the 1.4 driver (?)

It appears to happen unpredictably, which of course makes it mildly maddening. 

Happy to provide additional info or details, please let me know.

summary: Inserting documents with WriteConcern during failover broken
description:
Inserting a document during failover should throw exception unless you set w=0.

This fails as of https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/684
which was fixing w=0 inserts to _not_ throw exception - but it went a bit far and forgot to check the write concern after get_server().

There has not been a release of the driver since it broke, so it does not affect any current releases.

summary: Standalone connection to arbiter fails
description:
When connecting with a simple standalone connection string (ie. {{mongodb://hostname:port/}}) to an arbiter, the MongoClient constructor throws
{noformat}
Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'No candidate servers found' in test.php:11
{noformat}
This is in contrast to other drivers (eg. Python) and the {{mongo}} shell, which can make standalone connections to arbiters (usually for monitoring purposes).

It seems that the driver is applying the replica set logic for discovering servers, which is not appropriate for standalone connections.  Below is a verbose trace:
{noformat}

summary: Travis build status incorrect in v1.5 branch
description:
README.md in v1.5 contains
[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.org/mongodb/mongo-php-driver.png?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/mongodb/mongo-php-driver)

Which points to master, therefore causing incorrect build status indicator.

The CONTRIBUTING.md file says the branch is called 1.5, not v1.5

summary: Allow NumberLong numbers on 32-bit platforms if the number fits in the 32-bit int range
description: Right now, when any stored NumberLong is encountered when marshaling to PHP it is blocked on 32bit systems. Instead, it should be allows if the number fits in the 32bit int range.

summary: 32bit platform improvements
description: There are various tests that are failing on 32bit systems, and MongoCursor->info() and MongoCommandCursor->info() are also not usable on 32bit platforms due to the cursor ID being 64bit.

summary: Min server version check should use GTE comparison
description: The {{php_mongo_api_connection_min_server_version()}} function was introduced here: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/607/files#diff-c3dcc64c18e6676b8e04658080943b08R54

summary: MongoCursor::$timeout=-1 doesn't work anymore
description:
I upgraded MongoDB to 2.6 today and the PHP Driver to 1.5.1. Immediately after connection and before doing any queries I set MongoCursor::$timeout to -1. This used to prevent any timeouts in earlier versions. Now with the new MongoDB and driver I get:

MongoCursorTimeoutException, Read timed out after reading 0 bytes, waited for 30.00000 seconds

Now, when looking this up in the docs I find a (IMHO) highly confusing mix of different settings, deprecated settings and the like. I wonder:

1) Where does the default timeout of 30 seconds come from? Should there still be a default timeout with current versions at all? Where is the documentation for this?
2) How can I disable this globally (NOT for each query separately)? Interestingly, setting $timeout to a value other than -1 works as expected but yields a deprecation warning. -1 doesnt cause a deprecation warning - but doesn't work. I might, of course, set $timeout to a very high value but I'd rather get to the bottom of this.

summary: New Replica Set connections hang when secondaries are creating indexes during initialSync
php driver throws exception when connect string contains an unresolvable hostname
description:
When the mongodb connect string contains an unresolvable hostname, the php mongo driver v1.4.5 will print PHP warning, "Warning: MongoClient::__construct(): php_network_getaddresses: getaddrinfo failed: Name or service not known ".   It will proceed to work properly, so long as any seed host in the connect string is reachable.

In the 1.5.2 driver, it will throw an exception, like this:  exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'MongoClient::__construct(): php_network_getaddresses: getaddrinfo failed: Name or service not known' in ...

If the connect string contains a resolvable but unreachable hostname, then no error is generated at all (this is expected behavior).

The expected behavior should be that if any seed hosts are reachable, then MongoClient() connects successfully.    Otherwise, HA is compromised: if one of my secondaries has a bad DNS entry, then the entire replica set becomes unreachable.   

We have run into this problem in production when doing routine maintenance on a secondary which included changing its DNS name, causing the unintended consequence of bringing the entire replica set down.

summary: Explain how to_index_string() creates names for ensureIndex() and deleteIndex()
description: The [MongoCollection::deleteIndex()|http://php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.deleteindex.php] documentation, for all of its verbosity, doesn't explain that it simply appends "_1" to a string argument to delete an index by name. That would make it very clear as to why it is not suited to delete custom index names. Mentioning that {{ensureIndex()}} does the same for its string arguments would also allow users to realize the logic for both methods support string arguments (e.g. one could easily ensure/delete an index by simple field name, and an ascending index would be created and then deleted).

summary: Connection is not persitent with replica set and run on Linux
Latency in replica set connection in Linux mongo and PHP
description:
I have tested with 2 applications (C# and PHP) to send a request and get a response string from 5 servers(1 primary and 4 secondary) located in different continents using the same connection string (readPreference=secondaryPreffered).
For the C# application, I can get the response within 2-3 seconds, but for the PHP I get the same response within 12-15 seconds. So we know it's the PHP-mongo driver that causes the issue.
Package analyses reveal that in C# application, it first starts to grab the nearest secondary storage and then check for authentication; it later pings other servers to make sure they are alive before replying the client with a response. Meanwhile, in PHP application the implementation becomes totally different, it first grabs the server specified first in the connection string and verifies for authentication information even thought this first one is primary storage. Then it pings the next server to verify its status and again checks for its authentication with a request/reply of the client/server system information. This repeats until it completes all servers to finally pick the nearest one to send its response to.

Could someone please take a look into this because its optimization on replica set for PHP-mongo driver seems to have not been well done with thorough tests over remote servers ?

Regards,
-Adam ;)

The mongo driver fails to build as static extension
description:
The changelog doesn't mention anything about dropping PHP 5.3.x support. However, I was unable to make a successful build with 1.5.0, 1.5.2, and 1.5.3. The last stable version that builds successfully is 1.4.5.

The extension is built into the PHP binary by our build system since it's easier to deploy than using shared libs. A small script to reproduce the issue (under OS X):

#!/usr/bin/env bash

export PHP_AUTOCONF=autoconf213

wget http://us1.php.net/get/php-5.3.28.tar.bz2/from/this/mirror -O php-5.3.28.tar.bz2
wget http://pecl.php.net/get/mongo-1.5.3.tgz

tar -xf php-5.3.28.tar.bz2
tar -xf mongo-1.5.3.tgz

mv mongo-1.5.3 php-5.3.28/ext/mongo
cd php-5.3.28

./buildconf --force
./configure --enable-mongo
make

The build exits with this error:

main/internal_functions.c:71:2: error: use of undeclared identifier
      'phpext_mongo_ptr'
        phpext_mongo_ptr,
        ^
main/internal_functions.c:93:57: error: invalid application of 'sizeof' to an
      incomplete type 'zend_module_entry *[]'
        return php_register_extensions(php_builtin_extensions, EXTCOUNT TSRMLS...
                                                               ^~~~~~~~
main/internal_functions.c:89:25: note: expanded from macro 'EXTCOUNT'
#define EXTCOUNT (sizeof(php_builtin_extensions)/sizeof(zend_module_entry *))
                        ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
2 errors generated.
make: *** [main/internal_functions.lo] Error 1

Same thing happens under Ubuntu 12.04 with gcc 4.6.3.

summary: "Available since" documentation for options arrays
description:
It would be great to have "(since 1.4.0)"-type documentation for options accepted by MongoClient and MongoCollection methods, for example:

http://us3.php.net/manual/en/mongoclient.construct.php

It's not the least bit obvious which options are available as of the current stable release, which are documentation for the upcoming 1.5.0 release, and how long any of them have been available.

The docbook format does not seem to support versioning for options arrays, but really, anything would be better than what we have now. Simply manually appending "(since X)" to the end of each option's documentation would be preferable. description:

summary: Network Timeouts don't Blacklist a connection.
description:
Lightweight Repro Steps:
# Build replica set.
# Have Apache+PHP return simple page which reads from ReplicaSet
# Have one replica's network disappear, so all connections to it timeout.
# All further PHP requests wait for it to timeout before serving.

For this sample, I restarted Apache, then used curl to grab the page 15 times, pausing 5s between get's. After 5 iterations, I firewalled off the port for the third member of the replica set. The following are the times spent in PHP for each iteration:

Replica was blackholed via:
{noformat}

* Apache configured to run only a single PHP server.

summary: Allow count to work with query hints
MongoCursor::count() should use cursor's maxTimeMS
description: Related to PHP-1249 ({{count()}} doesn't use cursor's socket timeout). Slating for 1.6.x because the code overlaps closely with PHP-1160.

summary: MongoCollection::count() should throw MongoExecutionTimeoutException on maxTimeMS timeout
description: PHP-1160 add an {{$options}} argument to {{MongoCollection::count()}}, which means {{maxTimeMS}} may now be specified. Other command wrappers (e.g. aggregate, findAndModify) correctly throw a MongoExecutionTimeoutException for such a timeout, but legacy code in the count helper currently throws a general MongoException for any count error condition. This should be changed to be consistent with other helpers.

summary: PHP driver creates DBRef objects instead of DBPointer types
description:
If I'm not mistaken, the php driver create DBRef with { "$ref":, "$db":, "$id" }, instead of using the dbref type.

The problem is according to sanity check done by bson_validate() in the c driver, a document should not contain a key with $.

The result is that a document created with a php driver can not be modify with a c driver when it contains a DBRef type.

https://github.com/jeromelebel/MongoHub-Mac/issues/148
https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/CDRIVER-443

summary: Do not allow invalid limit values in MongoDeleteBatch
description:
The current write command spec states that 1 and 0 are the only valid values for the {{limit}} option of a delete command. Additionally, the option should always be specified.

[{{php_mongo_api_delete_add()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/a66c6c5c11b1825d32dd5dd194733a9a48e25e06/api/write.c#L392] only adds the {{limit}} option to the BSON command if the value is not -1. This should probably be changed so that it is always included.

[{{MongoWriteBatch::add()}}|http://php.net/manual/en/mongowritebatch.add.php] currently allows invalid values to be passed in. A user could supply a limit of 2, and the server will treat that as "no limit". If we decide to throw an exception for an invalid {{limit}} option, that might requiring version checking to be compatible with future server versions that will support specific limits.

At the very least, we should update the PHP documentation so users know that only 1 and 0 are acceptable options at this time.

summary: Rename 'delete' field to allow compilation with C++
description: A field in a structure defined in api/write.h is named "delete". This is a reserved keyword in C++ and breaks compilation. Please rename it to "deleter" or something else appropriate.

summary: Exception messages for invalid db/collection names are not helpful
description: calling ->selectCollection('') on a MongoCollection object results in a really cryptic error message "MongoDB::__construct(): invalid name" which is quite confusing because nowhere was MongoDB called and also I would've expected that MongoCollection constructs a MongoDB object not selectCollection.

description:
Last week, my webserver is very unstable. Because the following error:

Fatal error: Uncaught exception 'MongoConnectionException' with message 'Operation now in progress' in /class/Search.class.php:59 Stack trace: #0 /class/Search.class.php(59): Mongo->__construct('mongodb://myuser:...', Array) #1 /class/Search.class.php(38): Search->conn_mongo() #2 /code/part.php(22): Search->__construct('mongo') #3 {main} thrown in /class/Search.class.php on line 59

MongoDB Server looks normal at the same time.
I reported it before, and meet it again.
The same thing is I set profileinglevel with not 0.
After I run db.setprofilinglevel(0), everything goes well.
Is it a bug, or There are some limitations for profile?

summary: phpinfo() lists a default authentication mechanism (which may not be the default)
description:
{noformat}
                   Supported Authentication Mechanisms                   
MONGODB-CR (default) => enabled
SCRAM-SHA-1 => enabled
{noformat}


This is always printed.. Which is not correct as it depends on which MongoDB server you are connecting to.
We should probably remove that '(default)' or mention "default in 2.8+" "default in..."

summary: MongoDate->toDateTime is only supported for PHP 5.3.4 and later.
description:
According to package.xml, min php supported version is 5.3.0.

See RPM test build:
https://copr.fedoraproject.org/coprs/remi/morephp/build/59335/

ON RHEL-6, with PHP 5.3.3 when trying to load the extension:
PHP Warning:  PHP Startup: Unable to load dynamic library '.../mongo.so' - .../mongo.so: undefined symbol: php_date_initialize in Unknown on line 0

This API is exposed starting with 5.3.4 :(

summary: Validate encoding and decoding of MongoBinData::UUID_RFC4122
description:
The PHP driver provides two constants that can be used to force the driver to store a byte string as type UUID, MongoBinData::UUID and MongoBinData::UUID_RFC4122. It does not do any validation of the data to be stored as that type. This causes problems when data stored with the PHP driver is decoded by a driver that does full validation (python, java, .net). See PYTHON-787 as one example.

The PHP driver should at a minimum validate that the string is 16 bytes long, both when encoding and decoding.

http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4122.txt

summary: Windows build failure
Possible to read memory before mongo_cursor.ns pointer
bson_encode() segfaults with some BSON type classes
description:
{{bson_encode(new MongoDate())}} will segfault.

Implement all of the {{bson_encode()}} type tests (some are currently TODO items) and ensure they pass.

summary: bson_encode() does not handle MongoMaxKey and MongoMinKey
description: MongoMaxKey and MongoMinKey have empty BSON values and {{bson_encode()}} should return an empty string for both classes (as it does for {{null}}).

summary: bson_encode() MongoTimestamp erroneously creates BSON date
description: {{bson_encode(new MongoTimestamp())}} actually serializes a BSON date instead of a timestamp type. This also leads to PHP errors when {{bson.c}} attempts to access MongoDate properties, which do not exist on MongoTimestamp.

summary: Can not connect without authentication credentials (results in segfault)
description: Authentication is attempted even when no credentials are supplied, and on top of that, an error message is not correctly set, so we segfault when attempting to construct the exception message.

summary: No MongoBinData type validation

summary: Fix tests for 2.8
description:
There are a whole boatloat of tests that fail for 2.8, particularly when running against WiredTiger.

Looks like most of them are error message differences (why are we testing server error messages?!) or we making assumptions about the database internals - such as querying system.namespaces to see if a collection exists.

summary: MongoCollection::count() segfaults due to a double free
Add MongoDB::getCollectionInfo as per SPEC-54
Memory read errors with deprecated properties.
description: When running tests under valgrind with USE_ZEND_ALLOC=0, tests that touch the "connected" property of MongoClient fail with uninitialised memory reads.

summary: MongoCollection::ensureIndex() should not validate namespace length
MongoGridFS::remove() ignores justOne option when deleting chunks
MONGO_METHOD calls within generators cause segfaults
description:
Previously reported with {{MongoDB::selectCollection()}} in PHP-1231 and MongoCursor iteration in PHP-977.

The argument stack pushing done for MONGO_METHOD calls conflicts with how PHP executes generators. Refactoring out the MONGO_METHOD macros (PHP-820) would likely side-step the problem, but there may be another solution.

summary: Implement MongoCommandCursor::timeout()
Incorrect option name for connectionTimeoutMS
Support cursor in listCollections and listIndexes commands
Handle listCollections and listIndexes results for non-existent databases and collections
Unable to connect to replicaSet when first member is in startup status
description:
Hello,

I have a big issue with the php driver.
This is my connection string :
"mongodb://user:pwd@b1:27022,b3:27022";

When b1 is in STARTUP mode (rebuilding a huge index for instance after a restart) I'm not able to connect the database. 
(Therefore I use the replicaSet option set to the name of my replica, in the MongoClient constructor)
If I set b3 first in my connection string
"mongodb://user:pwd@b3:27022,b1:27022";
The driver success to connect to the replicatSet.

Thanks for your support,

Thomas.

summary: Do not enforce batch size for collection and index enumeration commands
description:
1.6.0-RC3 currently enforces a batch size of zero, which resulted in the first batch always being empty (highlighting the bug in PHP-1355).

For server 2.8.0-RC3, where cursor responses are optional (based on a {{cursor}} option being present), [this commit|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/35f5da4152179c0b8c1b091633499eca0def4c7a] included in PHP-1321 ensures that we handle both inline command and cursor responses. 2.8.0-RC4+ will always use cursor responses, regardless of whether the command document contains a {{cursor}} option (or a requested batch size). If we remove batch size enforcement, we can instead rely on the server to choose an optimal batch size.

Note: although we do have a {{MONGO_DEFAULT_COMMAND_BATCH_SIZE}} constant (defined as 101), that was made with aggregation in mind and may not necessarily be the same batch size used for collection and index enumeration cursors. The command spec is silent on this.

summary: Do not compare php_mongocursor_is_valid() result to FAILURE constant
Collection and index enumeration fails if cursor's first batch is empty
description: Discovered while testing 2.8.0-RC4. The existing code enforced a batch size of zero, which caused the first batch to always be empty.

summary: Command cursor may cause log_response_header callback to segfault
description: If the command cursor was constructed from a raw command result (e.g. {{MongoCommandCursor::createFromDocument()}}, it will not have a query. This will cause the {{log_response_header}} callback invocation to segfault.

summary: Allow command cursor option to be array or object
description: When invoking a raw command (using {{MongoDB::command()}}), the {{cursor}} option must be a BSON object if it is present; however, {{php_mongo_enforce_batch_size_on_command()}} requires it to be an array. This is problematic when the user wants to specify an empty BSON object as the cursor option (i.e. to allow the server's default batch size to be used), as an empty PHP array would not be serialized as an empty BSON object.

summary: php_mongo_runcommand() should load current element before handling errors
aggregateCursor() with $out does not force primary selection
description: The purpose of this issue is to make {{aggregateCursor()}} consistent with {{aggregate()}}; however, users will generally not want to use {{aggregateCursor()}} with pipelines ending in {{$out}}, since the command will not execute until the cursor is rewound and the result set will be empty anyway.

summary: aggregate() with var args does not restore read pref after forcing primary for $out
Force primary read pref over primaryPreferred if pipeline ends with $out
description:
The intention of forcing a primary read preference for pipelines ending in {{$out}} was the help users avoid a command error if a secondary were to receive the command. We also emit a warning when doing so in order to notify them that their code _could_ fail.

With primaryPreferred, it's still possible for the command to be sent to a secondary. Therefore, we should also force a primary read pref over primaryPreferred to be consistent.

summary: Clear tag sets when forcing primary read pref for aggregate
description:
When the aggregate command helpers detect {{$out}} in the pipeline and force a primary read pref, they only set the type. Any existing read preference tags are left in place, which causes {{mongo_filter_candidates_by_tagset()}} to disregard the primary if it does not match the tag sets.

Overriding the read pref for the collection ({{aggregate()}} or command cursor ({{aggregateCursor()}}) should never leave it in an invalid state. If we set a primary read pref, the tags should be cleared (even temporarily).

summary: explicitly null-terminate buffer written by gethostname()
description: Issue reported directly with a github PR: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/814

summary: Implement get/setReadPreference() for MongoCommandCursor
description: Additionally, the methods should be added to MongoCursorInterface.

Driver throw errors after replica-set primary switch
description:
We've setup a replicaset, 1 primary, 1 secondary and 1 arbiter; 
Server is configured with ssl.

Everything works fine before we do db switch.

Here're the steps:
a. On primary db server, I execute rs.stepDown(5) to make secondary sever promoted as primary.
b. Refresh page (with read and write) right after the above command.
c. I got following errors randomly:

   1. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL: Connection reset by peer
   2. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL: Broken pipe
   3. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL operation failed with code 1. OpenSSL Error messages: error:1409F07F:SSL routines:SSL3_WRITE_PENDING:bad write retry
   4. MongoClient::__construct(): SSL operation failed with code 1. OpenSSL Error messages: error:140940F6:SSL routines:SSL3_READ_BYTES:unknown alert type in ..php on line 14

I can understand that #1 and #2 can happen right after switch because php was still hold the old primary server's connection and result in connection lost after switch. 

But someone how all these 4 errors keeps happening after the switch and make the application unstable, especially the #3, this is the most frequent error.

summary: Core dump if name resolution is wrong for MongoDB servers
description:
We have noticed core dumps from time to time by PHP processes running MongoDB connections. I Suspected a transient DNS problem, and I managed to replicate this problem by deliberately breaking /etc/hosts. It is possible if you pass the wrong IPs for a hostname in /etc/hosts to cause the PHP MongoDB driver to dump core, at least under FPM in 5.3.

I have a three node replica set, A and B are full nodes and mgm is a arbitrator. All works fine with this in /etc/hosts:

10.99.99.151    mongodb-a.domain.tld
10.99.99.152    mongodb-b.domain.tld
10.99.99.150    mongodb-mgm.domain.tld

If I change this to this (broken) config:

10.99.99.150    mongodb-a.domain.tld
10.99.99.151    mongodb-b.domain.tld
10.99.99.152    mongodb-mgm.domain.tld

Every request results in a core file (core attached).

I am connecting to MongoDB using IP addresses only (mongodb://10.99.99.151, 10.99.99.151) but the MongoDB replica set is build using FQDNs.

I'm pretty sure that this is a bug, as while my name resolution is broken it should result in a can't connect error (or similar), not core fine. I'm guessing this has something to do with following a referral from the first node that is correctly connected, but i'm not really sure.

Thanks!

summary: New query options and response flags
description: Two new query options (AwaitData and Exhaust) and all new response flags (CursorNotFound, QueryFailure, and AwaitCapable) have been added to the Mongo wire protocol (http://www.mongodb.org/display/DOCS/Mongo+Wire+Protocol). The driver should handle the response flags appropriately (eg. CursorNotFound should throw exception rather than silently end the query results). The driver may implement the new query options if desired.

summary: Pin per-request connection to a secondary

summary: Mongo-enabled PHP scripts became unresponsive after failure in replica set SECONDARY
description:
We encountered a very strange behavior with the PHP driver. One of the secondaries in our replica set became unhealthy. Soon (but not immediately!) all PHP scripts connecting the replica set became unresponsive. They did not fail, but apparently timed out. As soon as the unhealthy secondary was removed from the replica set, all PHP scripts started to work properly.

Some miscellaneous facts:
* The failed secondary was not in the initial server list in PHP
* Rebooting the PHP processes or MongoDB servers did not help
* Connecting any of the servers over mongo cli worked well (this leads me to think this problem is in the PHP driver)

summary: MongoGridFSFile.getResource should specify 'stream' instead of 'resource' as return type
description:
This is the current document block for the function:

{noformat}
/**
 * This method returns a stream resource that can be used to read the stored file with all file functions in PHP.
 * The contents of the file are pulled out of MongoDB on the fly, so that the whole file does not have to be loaded into memory first.
 * At most two GridFSFile chunks will be loaded in memory.
 *
 * @link http://php.net/manual/en/mongogridfsfile.getresource.php
 * @return stream Returns a resource that can be used to read the file with
 */
{noformat}

Since 'stream' isn't a recognized php type this creates problems in IDEs that use type inference for autocompletion / code validation.

It should read :

{noformat}
/**
 * This method returns a stream resource that can be used to read the stored file with all file functions in PHP.
 * The contents of the file are pulled out of MongoDB on the fly, so that the whole file does not have to be loaded into memory first.
 * At most two GridFSFile chunks will be loaded in memory.
 *
 * @link http://php.net/manual/en/mongogridfsfile.getresource.php
 * @return resource Returns a resource that can be used to read the file with
 */
{noformat}

summary: Default writeConcern not write safe on standalone node
MongoDB::command with 'distinct' and without 'query' doesn't work anymore
Hostname matching with Replica Set doesn't work pre PHP 5.6
description:
PHP 5.6.0+ enables hostname matching by default.
This is done by automatically extracting the peer name from the hostname we are creating a connection to.

In PHP before 5.6.0 the user had to provide PHP with the hostname as part of the SSL context.

This hostname is singular. Its only one hostname -- the user cannot provide multiple, like for an entire Repilca Set.
Besides, the user shouldn't have to provide this value -- we do auto discovery of nodes, which may change on any given time, so for the user to provide the full list doesn't make a lot of sense.

summary: Add certification expiration matching
Connecting through SSL no longer works in PHP 5.6 with 2.4.13, 2.6.6, and later
description:
PHP 5.6 (incorrectly) redefined the meaning of STREAM_CRYPTO_METHOD_SSLv23_CLIENT.
Using SSLv23 now explicitly removes TLS support, leaving only SSLv2 and SSLV3 available.

MongoDB has never supported SSLv2, but did support SSLv3 until 2.4.13/2.6.6 which explicitly disabled SSLv3.

This leaves no available protocols in PHP 5.6.0-5.6.6.
This has been reported as https://bugs.php.net/bug.php?id=69195

summary: SSL peer verification should reference stream->context options
description: Currently, the {{capture_peer_cert}} option is read before the stream is created (from {{options->ctx}}). We should move that between stream creation and crypto setup, and have it access {{stream->context}} instead. Additionally, {{php_stream_context_get_option()}} calls after crypto setup should check {{stream->context}} instead of {{options->ctx}}.

summary: explain() does not raise appropriate exception for $err conditions
description:
Run {{explain()}} on a query which has no candidate plans (enable {{notablescan}}) and the result is a {{$err}} document (presumably the server result document).

Previous versions, notably 1.5.4, raise an exception for the same condition.

summary: Fix database name validation to include '$external', to allow execution of user admin commands
description:
See: http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/tutorial/control-access-to-mongodb-with-kerberos-authentication/#add-kerberos-principal-s-to-mongodb

Creating users for external auth mechanisms (e.g. Kerberos, LDAP) requires the ability to execute commands against the special {{$external}} database name. This is not possible given our current validation of database name arguments, as we throw an exception attempting to create a MongoDB instance with a {{$}} character.

Our current database validation is a mix of [Unix|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/limits/#Restrictions-on-Database-Names-for-Unix-and-Linux-Systems] and [Windows|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/limits/#Restrictions-on-Database-Names-for-Windows] naming restrictions, both of which disallow the dollar sign. A flexible solution would be to accept {{$external}} but still disallow other strings containing a dollar sign.
